review_text: This is an insanely good game, i was invested in the story so much and the way they reveal it slowly with each sol was perfect. The environment looks amazing and i was admiring the background in each area. Using manga panels to tell the story in each boss was so cool and unique, the art style was very appealing. Gameplay wise it was fun and challenging with 2 or 3 amazing boss fights. This is a must play and dare i say its better than hollow knight :P.
Hope you sharpened your parry skills.

hollow knight attacked and nine sols parried it.  and i say that as someone with 100% in both HK and silksong.  the combat is EXCEPTIONAL. not "good for an indie game". genuinely elite. parry-focused, sekiro like, weighty, punishing. every boss is a dance - whether its a tango or a slow waltz. you dont just react; you learn tells, delays, fake-outs, the game is absolutely brutal but fair. zero artificial grind.  the worst boss in nine sols is better than the best boss in most metroidvanias. true ending [spoiler]eigong[/spoiler] genuinely nearly broke me. for sure rivals some of the hardest fights ive ever done, but its absolute CINEMA.  the exploration is classic metroidvania done RIGHT. ability gates, hidden paths, secrets everywhere. you start weak, lost, overwhelmed, by the end, your practically immortal. the world is smaller than a map like silksong but denser.  the story really surprised me. revenge, atonement, world-class writing. the end had me seriously emotional. im not proud of it but the story earned it. nine sols has that unique ability to make you truly attached to the characters, in a way that is almost unparalleled.  the art style is one of the best ive seen. taoist sci-fi (chinese devs for context), hand-drawn, comic book cutscenes, the sound design is excellent – not metro level, but excellent nevertheless.  in all honesty, the skill curve is sharp and not entirely balanced, and some enemies feel rigged.  if you liked hollow knight, buy this. if you liked sekiro, buy this. if you like not being the guy who misses out on peak gameplay due to skill issues, buy this.  stop reading. your skill issue isnt an excuse. buy it.  9.7/10.

I really wish Steam allowed "mixed" reviews because the story, art, and world-building are all top notch. It just came down to too much of the game focusing on the parry mechanic. By the end of the game, the boss fights consist of 75% parrying, which should tell you whether this game will be for you or not. It's well crafted, but ultimately not for me.  I got as far as the final boss before putting it down.   If you enjoy parry-centric soulslike games, this will be up your alley. If that's not your thing and were coming for the metroidvania elements, I'd suggest staying away. Personally, I find the whole "miss one parry and lose 50% of your health" mechanic tiresome. Some people enjoy needing 50 attempts on a boss before winning, to each their own, but I don't have the time or the blood pressure for it.

At the time of this review I've just finished this game. I'm a big time metroidvania fan, and this one is definitely on my top 5.  It leans more into this new "soulsvania" subgenre, and excels on it's combat. It's very fast and reactive, giving you many tools to use, and having a very satisfying flow, similar to games like Sekiro, with a heavy parry focus.  Where it is a bit weaker compared to others is it's world and exploration, while still good, it's just not as strong, the levels are more self contained, and there are not that many gated locations for specific ability and items like this genre usually does. The art and the world design is top notch, mixing traditional Chinese elements with futuristic ones, creating a "Taopunk" feel, as described by the creators. The story and characters are really well written, and helps you get immersed in the world. Overall a solid experience.

Metroidvania vraiment excellent à tous points de vus. C'est une proposition très originale dans le genre "Taopunk" qui l'est tout autant   Là où le jeu brille le plus est son système de combats exigeant basé sur les parades  L'exploration n'est pas en reste et la narration est riche si on s'y intéresse   100% atteint en ~30h après un boss de fin magistral (et incroyablement difficile) qui met à l'épreuve tous les skills acquis au cours de l'aventure   Je souligne aussi la présence de visuels et des musiques magnifiques    Malgré cela, il faut noter un support wide screen (21:9) très mal fichu La difficulté du jeu peut être un gros point noir pour les joueurs casu, notamment avec l'absence d'I-frame qui provoque des situations où un coup encaissé peut entrainer 1 ou 2 autres coups supplémentaire inévitable et une mort très frustrante, le jeu est TRÈS exigeant dans ces combats  Je recommande tout de même cette pépite, c'est original et franchement une immense réussite

美术风格5/5：独特的道朋克美术风格真的很喜欢，人物、场景和UI设计风格非常统一，融合了传统美学和科幻的作品真的很少见。  关卡设计4.5/5：优秀的关卡设计，在跑图上没有遇到什么很难跑的地方，但在没有拿到传送前，跑路距离确实有点长，拿到传送后在炼丹区跑路也有点长……对我这种收集党也很友好，拿到地图芯片后也可以看哪里有漏东西可以补。  战斗4/5：自认为不算太手残了，但开了一般模式感觉战斗是真挺有难度，中间很想切剧情模式，但是告诉我拿不到成就我又忍了。有些精英怪难度堪比Boss。打精英死很多次，然后跑三百六十五里路真的折磨。强怪例如前期的枪兵角瑞，手巨长，我就是逃课用激光处理掉的；还有水鬼和隐月，无敌了的攻击欲望，完全招架不住，真的，有些怪太粪了。以及无量反击真的有点难用……  整体来说很不错，长板很长，也没有短板，各个方面都做的很优秀.
非常棒的游戏。流畅的战斗手感，想象力丰富的boss战演出，值得推敲的剧情设定，友好的复活点位置（没有365里路），共同打造了优秀的游戏体验。希望能有更多人知道并体验这款游戏

I am speechless. All the frustration of mastering the combat is made worth it by the incredibly rich story and flood of emotions you will feel when it all finally comes together. I have not cried this hard over a game since my first playthrough of Outer Wilds, and it might even be more than that. 打完了我太喜欢了，甚至是比空洞更对我胃口的一部作品。 赛博朋克+道教的世界观，以贴符弹反为核心的玩法，江湖志怪的画风以及配乐都完全是我的好球区。一般模式30h全成就，可能是我太爱弹反了所以基本上没卡过关我觉得不算难。支线也都做得很好，每个npc都塑造得有血有肉很立体。尤其是主角羿，太帅了羿大人膜拜膜拜。。 地图设计可能略显简单但是探索物品和正反馈给得很足，大小战斗的节奏把控也恰到好处，每个地区每个boss战都配有不同的主题乐器。剧情方面我真的很喜欢，科幻和道教哲学、欲望和道德的碰撞。每一位boss都有参考原型，真结局打到最后才明白为什么游戏会叫九日，为什么主人公会叫羿。何谓后羿射日，十日归一。 玩得很开心。很喜欢。推荐喜欢只狼+类银的所有人去玩！

I enjoyed everything about this game; The world, the art, the characters (especially the main character's development), gameplay, pretty much everything about this game I pretty much did not have any problems with.  The map marked where to go some times which made going from point A to point B a little more direct than Hollow Knight. Outside of those cases, you may have to rely on your memory to remember certain points of the map to come back later when you have the abilities to advance to the next area.  The parry system in this game and the mechanics relative to it were all super satisfying to use. The bosses were challenging and, at least for me, enjoyable. You do not need to study any complex readings, rather you can find patterns in their attack and... parry. Making mistakes is quite punishing so I always felt like piece of sh#t whenever I mistimed by parries or run into a very obvious attack.   I recommend this game if anyone likes parrying and Hollow Knight. This game might be easier than it though.

个人打分七分，其实是想给不推荐的，毕竟也打了三十小时了，还是给个好评吧 地图探索一般，平平无奇，探索完一个地图奖励你一个大boss，不过梦境那关的地图倒是有点意思。除此之外没啥记忆深刻的点 战斗方面个人体验不是很爽，基本全是背板boss，要么秒死，要么满血过，容错率很低，被打中一次，boss一套连击就下来了，直接躺平回家。连个小怪都和多动症一样，进攻欲望贼强。打得比丝之歌还痛苦，丝之歌后期我直接掏出漫游者大人，无脑砍砍砍，基本没有卡关的 最后打易公前还把我npc杀了，把地图埋了，传送禁了，现在是打也打不过，钱也没地方挣，化伤玉也拿不了。拿着我四瓶药和7格血死撑 说到钱，为什么现在游戏一个个都喜欢整死亡扣钱，最近玩的几个类银，全是死亡扣钱的，其他俩游戏好歹还有个死亡不掉落的绑定钱，九日是只要死了就没钱。我在这游戏掉了也有几万块钱了，整的我啥也买不起。好不容易花了几千块买了个毒物，想加个血条上限，结果发现npc死了 剧情方面还可以，漫画的形式让人眼前一亮，但是主角的性格有点不喜欢，傲慢固执还有点大男子主义，前期行事也是疯批属性拉满，太残忍了以至于有些接受不了，后期倒是改变了很多

纯粹的战斗爽银河城，与其说银河城更不如说2d只狼like。地图看着大，但是地图设计的非常一般，整个地图充斥着大量的单行道和回头路，游戏里的小地图甚至还没有图钉功能，主打一个神秘。游戏的重心主要放在战斗交互上，三种不同类型的符咒，20个左右的玉石构成的build完全撑得起这个游戏的流派构筑，不同符咒和玉石的构筑带来的战斗思路是完全不同的：行云流水主打见缝插针，一气贯通主打利用贴符补给的弓箭资源管理与boss交互，收放自如则是主打配合无惧玉和boss换血。可惜受限于成本，整个游戏值得好好构筑研究不同打法的boss实在不多，大多boss受限于流程设计的较为简单，一周目利用格挡背板便能较为容易的打过，只有最终boss设计的血厚复杂且变招多，不同构筑的流派衍生的不同打法在这时候才能派得上用场，而其他boss一周目通关也没能提供一个加强版供玩家研究构筑，只能说遗憾。但是一周目打下来体验还是比较丝滑的，战斗系统虽然有点训狗但是是撑得起一周目的战斗爽体验，值得推荐。

I'll start by admitting that I'm a bit out of my depth here. Neither 2D action-platformers nor metroidvanias are genres I tend to enjoy. However, I do enjoy soulslikes and an interesting narrative.  So, on a whim, I decided to give Nine Sols a shot. A few hours in, and I wasn't really feeling it. The navigation wasn't all that interesting, and the first boss had such a steep difficulty spike that it felt artificial. I didn't really vibe with the tone, and the dialogue-heavy segments felt a bit childish and slow. I even put the game down for a few days, contemplating whether I should press on or give up.  Luckily, I pressed on. Because oh man, was I wrong.  My initial impressions of the tone were completely subverted. I became deeply invested in the lore and the unfolding story, even if I didn't enjoy every dialogue sequence equally. The enemy variety and complexity became much more interesting, and both the navigation and level design grew on me.  The combat leans heavily into parrying mechanics, though you do have a wider toolset that, when used effectively, makes for some really fun and rewarding fights. At times though, enemies and attacks can become a bit spammy, and it occasionally feels like the game breaks its own rules.  I really liked the character progression, which ties into both combat and exploration, even if the skill tree felt more like a checklist than a way to build a unique character.  The narrative beats, the visual presentation, and the excellent music; that's really what kept me going. Even through some insane difficulty spikes with certain bosses, I pressed on for the sake of discovery. The hand-drawn art and "Taopunk" aesthetic really grew on me. It's dark, brutal, contemplative and beautiful.  I recommend Nine Sols.

스토리 전개가 시원시원해서 좋습니다. 대만에서 만든 게임 답게 가차없다고 해야하나. 어물쩡하게 넘기는 부분 없이 복수, 속죄, 식민지배의 착취구조, 디아스포라 등등의 주제들을 메트로바니아 게임이라는 시스템 안에서 적절히 다룬 사이버펑크물입니다. 거기에다가 도교적인 요소까지 넣어서 종교의 의미에 대해서도 생각할 수 있었던 것 같아요. (타오 펑크라는 새로운 장르를 잘 개척한 것 같습니다)  그리고 스토리모드가 있어서 저 같은 겜 존못도 할 수 있습니다. 스토리모드여도 맵은 어김없이 다 돌아야하지만 그정도는 겜 존못도 노력하면 할 수 있는 부분이네요.   재밌었어요. 덤으로 오빠랑 여동생이 수상할 정도로 사이가 좋습니다.

Absolutely peak fiction, i have no words for how beautiful this game was. Except i will try to do so, this game is both emotionally engaging and just the right amount difficult.   I found myself unable to settle for the regular ending and played again just to get the true ending, that is how engaged in the characters and story i was. The gameplay is very fun, the obstacles and puzzles were not infuriatingly difficult nor stupidly easy.   The boss fights.. oh man the boss fights, I have played the final boss at least forty times and done the others at least five in battle memories. The regular mobs gradually scale in difficulty in a way that is noticeable yet very well timed to not let you be bored.   The art and theme have really nailed a genre i didn't even know existed yet wish i had, beautiful and beautiful.   All in all 20/10, would play and will play eighty more times.

[h3] 2D横スクアクションの死にゲー！パリィが重要！ [/h3]  パリィ主体のゲームを探していた時に出会えた作品。 ノーマルモードで1周目をクリアしたのでレビュー。  とにかく死にまくる。後半のボス戦は特に。序盤は雑魚敵ですら何回も死んで攻撃を覚える必要がある。 完璧なタイミングでのパリィや回避を連続して求められるので、SEKIROよりもはるかに難しかった。 回復薬を飲むタイミングでさえ見極める必要がある。 しかし、このひりつく難易度が絶妙に面白いし、何回も挑戦してクリアした時の達成感が心地よかった。  [h3] ＜難易度＞ [/h3] 難易度は以下2種類から選択可能。 ①ストーリーモード：ストーリーメインで進めたい人やアクションゲーム不慣れな人用の難易度。 ②ノーマルモード　：上記以外の人用であるが、他のゲームのハードに匹敵する難易度。全然ノーマルではない。 他の3Dアクションゲームに比べてできる事が少ないので、単調であるが故に余計に難しく感じた。  [h3] ＜パリィの役割＞ [/h3] 本作品ではパリィをしても敵の体幹ゲージは削れない（そもそもHPゲージしかない）。 その為、どれだけ敵の攻撃をパリィし続けようが、プレイヤーが能動的に攻撃をしなければ敵を倒す事ができない。 つまり、パリィは攻撃する隙を作る為の一つの行動にすぎない事に注意。 とは言っても、他の防御手段としてはクールタイム有の回避とジャンプぐらいなので、 敵の猛攻を上手くパリィできるかどうかが攻略の決め手となる事が多かった。  [h3] ＜最後に＞ [/h3] この絶妙な難易度のゲームは他では中々味わえないと思う。 何回死んでも逆に燃えてくるような、難しいアクションゲームを探している人にオススメです。

[h3]ソウルライク+メトロイドヴァニアの傑作[/h3] [list] [*]真エンドクリアまで20時間程。 [*]慣れるまでは難易度やや高め。 [*]分岐・時限有で全実績解除まで2週必須で30時間↑ [/list] [hr][/hr] 道教をベースにしたSF色の強い世界観、完成度の高いシナリオとBGM 美しいビジュアルに、SEKIROを彷彿とさせるパリィ主体の手応えのあるアクション… どの要素も高水準にまとまっていて個人的には神ゲーと言っても差し支えない出来でした。  最初は厳しく感じるのに「行ける…行けるぞ！」と段々何かを掴み始めるあの快感！ 特にボス戦は本当に楽しく、猛攻をパリィで防ぎきった時の気持ち良さは格別です。 2Dゆえに情報量が少ない為か、この難易度でも不思議な事に遊びやすいバランスに感じました。  メトロイドヴァニア系のゲームの中でも本作は仲間キャラの掘り下げやサブイベが豊富で、 ストーリーの説明もしっかりしていて世界観やキャラを理解しやすい点も好印象でした。  良作を生み出し続けているインディーゲームスタジオらしいので今後の作品にも期待大です！  ■[b]分岐・時限実績[/b]  分岐のある唯一のサブイベの実績が厄介でこの為だけに2週必須なのは少し不親切に感じました。 時限実績も結構あって1個見逃したまま1週目クリアまで行っていしまいました。 とはいえ2週目完走の必要は無く全プレイヤーの全実績解除率も15%程で簡単な方かと思います。

Fantastic Game! I would say it's pretty similar to Hollow Knight, only better in pretty much every aspect: - better artstyle - better characters - better fast-travel-system/less backtracking - way better upgrade/progression system - much, much better fighting-mechanic. I found the fighting in HK pretty boring, just hit everything two or three times and done. In Nine Sols it's more complex and much more challenging and fun. There are three different parries, and it's really important to master them. While for most regular enemies dodging is enough, for the bosses you absolutely need to master at least the first kind of parry or you won't stand a chance. Which brings us to the next point: - fantastic and creative boss fights that are on par with the ones from from software games, gameplaywise and difficultywise. While I beat the final boss in HK on first try, the final boss in Nine Sols took me more than three hours. But it was still great fun. They absolutely mastered the formula from From Software: at first, most bosses wipe the floor with you pretty fast, you don't stand a chance. But than you get better, start learning their attack patterns, learn to time you parries right, and you feel like you can beat him now, but somehow end up dying anyway. But then, when you finally beat him, you feel like you really accomplished something. And then the second phase begins, and the cycle starts again...  So while I absolutely loved Nine Sols and woul highly recommand it, you should be aware that, in standard mode, it is a rather difficult game, especially the boss fights are quite challenging. There is, however, a story mode that is easier. I haven't tried it myself, but it should give players the opportunity to enjoy Nine Sols, even when they're not into difficult games

Nine Sols is such a beautiful game. I am about 12 hours in and i havent't even scratched the surface. The music, art design, story, combat, all of them feels just right. Specially the music, when a boss fight happens the music bangs. I'll probably buy their music pack after i finish the game.  Now coming to the art design , it is so pretty. It feels like reading a colored manga book and they give bits and pieces of black and white manga pages here and there to show the tension of the conversation. For combat, most of the opponents are a breeze when you get the hang of parry and talisman mechanic but there are a few skill check side bosses here and there.  For level design, this game intimidates me. There are so many different ways leading to different paths which somehow gets connected to each other. Now the only thing i don't like is how the game don't allow me to progress in a area because i didn't have a skill required to get through that and i get that skill much later in the game so i have to remember exactly where that area was and travel all the way back through multiple points on foot since there is no fast travel. The map was also hard to figure out at first but you get used to it.  Now other than these the game is a masterpiece is so far and i will absolutely finish it. It deserves the same love other indie titles like hollownight and cuphead got. So far i absolutely recommend this game to you if you are into story rich good combat games

A história também surpreende bastante e vai ficando cada vez mais interessante conforme o jogo avança.  É um jogo difícil, mas justo. Quando você aprende os padrões dos inimigos e finalmente vence um chefe complicado, a sensação recompensa totalmente o esforço. A phenomenal game with a hauntingly beautiful soundtrack. immersive world building, bold and unique characters, and a story that is both horrifyingly desolate and deeply comforting. As a Hollow Knight fan I had my eye on this game for a while and I knew I had to play it, and Nine Sols did not disappoint. Every boss (and miniboss) truly feels like a culmination of the abilities or enemies you have learned up until their fight. Not once did I finish a boss fight feeling like it was unfair. I did have to check attack animation tips on two separate occasions but other than that I didn't have to use any battle walkthroughs.  I also have to say that the sound design is so so so lovely. Atmospherically there is much artificiality in terms of sfx (as would be expected considering the setting) but it makes the parry sound and the instruments and vocals in the soundtrack stand out so much more. The soundtracks themselves utilize vocals and rhythmic electronica synths in a bold and addictive mix and I've had several of the boss themes (and an overworld ost) on loop in my playlist since I heard them. It takes a lot for a game to be able to reach the heights this one does. I always dreaded after playing Ori, I wouldn't find another metroidvania (or any game for that matter) that would spark the same feeling that game gave me. Nine Sols is one of those games that has actually managed to strike that hyper specific chord Ori did.   The gameplay, top notch, like Sekiro but 2d, and that's my 2nd fav Soulsborne after Elden Ring. The story? Incredibly engaging, the protaganist actually feels like he holds up to his high status, unlike some other games. The world? I want more of it, its so rich, and I love it. The characters? All loveable. The music? ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ the music makes me euphoric, the OST is so perfect. This is Taopunk at its best, for sure. Personal GOTY for 2024, even if I only got to this in 2026. I also adore how this game does not stray away from deep, dark themes and horrifying imagery of some of the games later content, its such a breath of fresh air. And the final boss? Actually perfect, I'm so sick and tired of final bosses now-a-days being an absolute cakewalk, that you can just stomp all over without it testing you on the things you learned throughout the game before hand. Genuinely one of my biggest pet peeves about games now is that the final boss isn't nearly as hard as some other, random boss you find in a completely miss-able side area, with some obscure way of getting there.  If you have the money extra, get this game, you will not regret it. I sure didn't :D

Este es un juegazo que no entiendo como no es más conocido, es un metroidvania con mecánicas de parries ¿qué más querés papá?  Una de las cosas que más me gustó del juego fue el pabellón y los PNJs que están ahí, ver como el pabellón va cambiando mientras más avances en el juego es increíble y además me encanta que podas progresar en las misiones de los PNJs trayendo objetos o progresando en la historia, especialmente con Shuashuan. También me gusta como se cuenta la historia, porque a diferencia de los Souls-like que tenés que leer todas las descripciones de los objetos y rescatar de los poco de historia que te dan los juegos, este directamente te lo van contando los mismos personajes por medio de diálogos, y esta buenísimo porque conoces Lore, la relación de los personajes, motivaciones y sus personalidades al mismo tiempo. Y por ultimo la jugabilidad es muy satisfactoria, literalmente te convertís en un objeto inamovible cuando perfeccionas los controles. Los jades son objetos que se parecen mucho a los amuletos del Hollow Knight, aunque no cambian mucho el gameplay igual me gustaron. En conclusión, es un 9/10 (no te podes detonar a la Lady Etherea, Goumang ni Ji)

재미있었다. 특유의 패링시스템이 신선하게 다가온 게임이었다. 보스도 마지막구간정도만 빼면 그렇게 어렵게 설계해놓지 않아서 편안하게 즐긴 느낌이다.  맵구성도 샅샅히 찾지 않고 설렁성렁 돌아다녀도 거의 대부분 마스터 할 수 있다는 것도 어쩌면 장점이다. 그 대신 잡몹이라도 그냥 맞으면서 했다가는 골로가는 데미지를 가지고 있긴 하니까 맵이 쉬운대신 몬스터가 어려운 느낌이다. 나중에 패링을 잘 할 수 있게 되면 별 문제 없어지지만.  이 게임 맵핑 기능이 없길래 약간 불안했었는데 없는 이유가 있었다. 맵핑기능이 필요할 정도로 어렵게 꼬아둔 맵이 아예 없다. 나중에 갈 도교 석굴이 그나마 어려운 퍼즐맵인 편. 하다보년 내가 이 구역에서 못 먹은 아이템이 몇개 인지 숫자로 표기만 해주는 이유가 있었다. 그냥 맵을 다 밝히면 다 먹을 수 있게끔 설계 해놓았다. 그 덕에 다른 메트로바니아 게임처럼 숨겨진 아이템을 찾는 재미는 살짝 반감되는 편이다.  내가 지금까지 해 본 메트로바니아 게임 중에서는 쉬운 편에 속한다. 쉬운 맵, 그냥 설렁설렁 돌아다녀도 다 먹을 수 있는 물자와 설정들, 그와 대비되는 패링시스템을 적극요구하는 살벌한 몬스터들이 서로 균형을 이루는 느낌이다. 그렇다고 몬스터 하나 잡는데 오래 걸리는 것은 아니고,  패링에 자신있는 사람들은 쉽다고 느낄 난이도였다. 보스 들도 특색있는 편이니까 신선한 매트로바니아 게임을 찾는다면 한 번 쯤 해볼만 하다.  단점으로 이 게임 번역이 상당히 부실했다. 오타도 있고, 존댓말을 했다가 반말을 하는 등, 번역에 아쉬운 느낌이 강했다. 알아보지 못할 정도는 아니지만 ,  가끔 고개를 기웃하게 될 정도. 그리고  이 게임 공포요소와 고어한 요소가 의외로 상당하다. 2d 게임에서 무서우면 얼마나 무섭겠어? 했다가 의외로 무서운 점이 있어서 놀랐다. 중간에 점프스퀘어가 대놓고 있기도 하니까 주의. 고어한 부분도 상당히 있으니 [ 폭사라던가, 잘린 시체들 같은것이 적나라하게 표현되는 정도임] 주의하자.  솔직히 내 플레이 타임중 8시간은 마지막 보스였다. 여기만 ㅅㅂ 하드코어 난이도다 ㄹㅇ  실크송 막보보다 살짝 더 어려운 느낌.  하지만 재밌었죠? 다들 츄라이 츄라이.

Шикарная игра. Явно не то, чего я ожидал от игры на первый взгляд. Шикарный лор; шикарный трогательный и жестокий сюжет; шикарные, зачастую неоднозначные персонажи (даже гг, которому моментами въе6ать хотелось, но и без этого он твaрь та ещё); шикарный геймплей, визуал и саундтрек, особенно трек в титрах, который после тру концовки воспринимается иначе.  Очень надеюсь, что разрабы в будущем приоткроют завесу прошлого. Сама история закончена, это да, но есть персонажи, о прошлом которых было бы очень интересно узнать побольше. Взять к примеру Цзи и фрагмент его жизни при Лаоцзы. Лично мне кажется, что это было бы довольно интересно.  В целом игра не сказать что идеальная, иногда возникают вопросы (чаще всего к странноватым на некоторых боссах хитбоксам), но всё же остался именно приятный осадок. Так было обидно, что эта история закончилась... Но ведь именно это и есть признак хорошей игры.  [b]Так что тут и для тех кто любит подобный геймплей  – найдется че-нибудь интересное.[/b] [b]Для тех, кто хотел бы просто разобраться в лоре и посмотреть сюжет – есть «Сюжетный режим».[/b] Я лично не чекал, но предполагаю что даже у новичков жанра там проблем не возникнет, что позволит просто кайфануть от истории.  Я бы хотел расписать оооооочень много об этой игре, о своих впечатлениях, догадках, о персонажах, о сюжете, но это займёт СЛИШКОМ много времени, да и мысли структурировано я выражать не умею, получится каша. Вкратце: я в восторге.    [spoiler]Лучше не звоните Солу[/spoiler]

Nine Sols é uma daquelas experiências que conseguem ir muito além de um excelente jogo de ação. O maior destaque está na sua narrativa: uma história complexa, envolvente e cheia de camadas, que mistura ficção científica, mitologia, filosofia e temas profundos como memória, destino, sacrifício e o peso das escolhas.  Desde os primeiros momentos, o jogo cria uma atmosfera de mistério que prende o jogador. A construção do mundo é excepcional, com uma quantidade impressionante de detalhes espalhados pelos cenários, diálogos e registros encontrados durante a jornada. Nada parece estar ali por acaso; cada descoberta ajuda a montar o grande quebra-cabeça sobre a civilização, os personagens e os acontecimentos que moldaram aquele universo.  Os personagens são um dos maiores pontos fortes. Eles possuem motivações próprias, conflitos internos e histórias que fazem o jogador realmente se importar com o que acontece com eles. A relação entre o protagonista e os demais personagens é construída com cuidado, trazendo momentos emocionantes que dão ainda mais significado à aventura.  A jogabilidade também merece destaque. A mistura de exploração estilo metroidvania com um sistema de combate baseado em precisão e defesa cria uma experiência desafiadora, mas extremamente satisfatória. As batalhas exigem atenção e aprendizado, fazendo cada vitória parecer realmente conquistada.  A direção de arte é simplesmente fantástica, combinando estética oriental com elementos futuristas de uma forma única. Cada área tem identidade própria e transmite perfeitamente o clima da narrativa. A trilha sonora complementa tudo com músicas marcantes, capazes de intensificar tanto os momentos de tensão quanto os momentos mais emocionais.  *Nine Sols* é um jogo que mostra como o gênero pode contar histórias profundas sem perder a qualidade do gameplay. Uma jornada memorável, com um universo rico, personagens marcantes e uma narrativa que permanece na mente mesmo depois dos créditos. Uma recomendação fácil para quem aprecia jogos com alma e propósito.

只谈游戏，玩起来很不错，是玩完后能体会到余韵的好作品。 剧情方面我很喜欢，前中后期的剧情都没落入俗套，给足了我新鲜感和探索欲，r18g的表达方式我很喜欢，血腥的描写和残酷的氛围很称，主角的人设也很不错，整体都很爽快。立意也有深度。 操作方面我觉得还行，游戏玩法建立在弹反上，由弹反获得资源去使用伤害技，长按松手触发弹反也挺简单，玉石系统聊胜于无吧，没护符那种质变。通关后有神居，没啥动力打了。 一周目直接通关真结局，15小时，支线做完重要物品清完，时长和体量感觉很适合，差不多在要腻的时候结束了，打完后百感交集，哎，是个好游戏。 后羿射十日，道归自然。

[h1]Il cammino spirituale della lince errante[/h1]  Da molto tempo avevo adocchiato [i]Nine Sols[/i] l'intenzione ci comprarlo, e quando finalmente l’ho preso, qualche settimana fa, non immaginavo che sarebbe diventato uno degli acquisti più soddisfacenti della mia vita videoludica.  [h3]Trama 🪢 l'intreccio di vendetta e destino[/h3]  Il gioco si apre in [i]medias res[/i]: dopo essere stato sconfitto da una figura misteriosa, Yi, il protagonista, precipita da un dirupo. Passano secoli prima che un bambino, suonando un flauto, lo risvegli dal suo sonno forzato. Con il suo ritorno alla vita riaffiora anche il suo desiderio di vendetta: non solo verso chi lo ha abbattuto, ma verso tutti coloro che hanno abusato del proprio potere e portato la corruzione nel mondo. Il nostro compito è accompagnarlo nella sua missione: trovare i Nove Sol, i responsabili della caduta dell’ordine, e decidere il destino di un’intera civiltà.  E tuttavia, il gioco non rivela subito le sue intenzioni: dopo un preludio fatto di tranquillità apparente, Yi decide che è il momento di agire davvero. La storia prende ritmo, e da lì in avanti il pacing è sorprendentemente equilibrato. La trama si svela poco alla volta, in modo regolare, senza mai risultare né affrettata né diluita, e non mancano i colpi di scena inaspettati.  Un elemento che ho amato particolarmente sono le scene in stile fumetto, che aggiungono intensità emotiva e visiva ai momenti chiave della storia. Sono curate, dinamiche, e danno alla narrazione un’identità ancora più forte.  [h3]Personaggi 💬 incontri voluti dal destino[/h3]  [i]Nine Sols[/i] è ricco di personaggi con i quali possiamo interagire. Ogni incontro, anche il più breve, aggiunge un tassello al mondo di gioco e al percorso interiore di Yi. Che si tratti di alleati, NPC o avversari imponenti, ognuno di loro ha una voce, un ruolo, e un frammento di storia: non sono mai semplici comparse. I Sol, soprattutto, sono davvero “Sol”: figure di potere, gloria, violenza… ognuno con un design che racconta la sua storia prima ancora che apra bocca.  Tra tutti, mi vorrei soffermare su due figure:  Yi 😼  È un protagonista dalle mille sfaccettature, non un semplice avatar vuoto. Parla, sente, soffre… è un personaggio completo, con un passato doloroso, un presente incerto e un futuro che dipende dalle nostre scelte. Le sue interazioni con gli altri personaggi rivelano un carattere forte, determinato, ma anche vulnerabile. È raro trovare un protagonista così ben scritto.  Shuanshuan 💜  È il cuore emotivo del gioco. Con lui si può instaurare, se lo si desidera, un rapporto di amicizia profonda e sincera. Yi, abituato a vivere da solo e a fidarsi solo di sé stesso, trova in questo bambino umano una figura che lo guarda senza paura, che vuole imparare e che vede in Yi un amico e un maestro. E in cambio, Shuanshuan gli restituisce qualcosa che Yi aveva dimenticato di avere: una famiglia.  [h3]Ambientazione e colonna sonora 🎆 l'armonia tra passato e futuro[/h3]  Gli ambienti di [i]Nine Sols[/i] sono uno dei punti forti che catturano subito lo sguardo. [i]Nuova Kunlun[/i], il mondo che esploriamo, è un misto di templi antichi e circuiti elettrici, di pergamene e ologrammi. È il manifesto perfetto del [i]Taopunk[/i], un’estetica che fonde la spiritualità del Taoismo con la brutalità scintillante della tecnologia.  Ogni zona ha una propria identità visiva, costruita con cura maniacale, con colori dominanti, architetture riconoscibili unite a simboli che raccontano la storia del luogo. Si passa da quartieri futuristici illuminati da neon accecanti a villaggi che sembrano usciti da un dipinto tradizionale cinese.  È un mondo che invita a fermarsi. Io l’ho fatto decine di volte: per guardare un panorama e tutti i suoi dettagli, per scattare uno screenshot come ricordo.   E mentre si esplora, la musica accompagna il viaggio. Ogni zona ha un tema unico, perfettamente cucito a regola d'arte e a seconda della situazione: si passa da melodie lente e tranquille a ritmi più veloci e aggressivi per le fasi di combattimento. La colonna sonora unisce strumenti tradizionali orientali, come i flauti e i tamburi rituali, a beat elettronici e sonorità moderne. Il risultato è un mix sorprendente che fa dialogare passato e futuro.  [h3]Gameplay ☯️ l'arte della parata perfetta[/h3]  [i]Nine Sols[/i] è un metroidvania puro, ma con un’anima tutta sua: la parata. Schivare è sicuramente utile, ma il gioco ti insegna che il vero spirito del combattimento sta nell'affrontare il nemico a testa alta, leggere i suoi pattern e parare ogni colpo con precisione assoluta. È come un addestramento nelle arti marziali: si parte dalle basi, si cresce, si sbaglia, si cade, si riprova… e alla fine si diventa maestri.  Il sistema di abilità e accessori permette di personalizzare lo stile di gioco e adattarsi anche ai boss più ostici. Personalmente, ho provato diverse  combinazioni fino a trovare quella perfetta per me. Mi è molto piaciuto vedere, soprattutto, come Yi utilizzi il [i]Qì[/i], l’energia vitale del Taoismo, per combattere e piegare a sé la tecnologia futuristica.  Anche dopo decine di morti, posso dire di non essermi annoiato neanche un istante. Ho sempre avuto lo stimolo ad andare avanti e a migliorare, fino a diventare intoccabile.  [h3]Conclusione 💘 un dardo nel cuore[/h3]  Dopo 40 ore di gioco meravigliose, e un finale toccante che mi ha distrutto e lasciato un nodo alla gola, mi sento svuotato e pieno allo stesso tempo. Dire che mi è “piaciuto” sarebbe riduttivo: l’ho amato, l’ho adorato, l’ho fatto mio.  [i]Nine Sols[/i] è uno dei videogiochi più belli che abbia mai giocato. Si percepisce chiaramente che gli sviluppatori ci hanno messo l’anima, il cuore e una passione immensa. Ogni dettaglio, dalla narrazione al gameplay, dalle animazioni alla musica, trasmette dedizione, cura e amore, creando un capolavoro meraviglioso.  Grazie, [b]Red Candle Games[/b].

Nine Sols gets a lot of comparison to Hollow Knight but aside from aesthetics and similar control schemes, I'm not sure that I would fully agree.  It feels more accurate to say that it's the Sekiro to Hollow Knight's Dark Souls 1. While it doesn't quite meet the immersion and exploration highs of Hollow Knight, it still offers a solid and unique world. Each area has a distinct vibe and aesthetic, and fits into the overarching narrative well. Nothing feels that hard to find (which is a common detractor of Metroidvanias for some), but exploration is still fun and backtracking is made incredibly easy about halfway through the game.  Tonally, Nine Sols is grittier and darker than I initially expected, though this was a welcome surprise. The protagonist Yi is jaded (pun intended) and ruthless, and we get to unpack his baggage and see him grow as the game progresses. All of this is bolstered by an amazing art style and soundtrack (credits theme especially).  Where Nine Sols really shines is the combat, which is a more challenging, parry-based alternative to Hollow Knight. Once you acclimate to the controls, everything feels buttery smooth and incredibly satisfying. Boss fights are high quality, with the final boss being one of my favorite bosses ever. And even if the combat isn't for you, options are available to reduce difficulty according to your preferences.  All of these things packed into one game make Nine Sols a unique and tight experience that I couldn't recommend enough! (You also heal by smoking a pipe and increase your max health by getting wasted, if you weren't convinced by now).

Normally I get upset at games like Sekiro and Dead Cells, but this game is so genuinely unique, that in even the most frustrating of moments, I can't help but appreciate the beauty in this game. Each boss fight that seems hard, with patience, can be figured out, and while the game is hard on the surface level, they really take the time to make sure you master basic mechanics, while in other games, you must figure them out on your own. It reminds me a lot of Hollow Knight, but with specialized gimmicks for each area. Some areas, there's acid pools, others, there robots, in some you must parry, in others you must charge forward. Each enemy provides wonderful pattern recognition, a few of every enemy attack going into the boss fights themselves, so mastering fights with the weaker enemies really helps. This game is so genuinely unique in a way that not even I can find myself getting angry. Frustrated, sure, but then I can only admire the complexity of each movement. Not to mention all of the unique accompanying sound effects. A truly wonderful game indeed.

[h1]Nine Sols — удачный микс идей разных жанров.[/h1]  После предательства своей наставницы таинственный фанши по имени «И» выживает, намереваясь отомстить 9 Солам — грозным правителям технологической инопланетной расы, остановив эпидемию, которая погубила народ солярийцев, и обретя мир в Новый Куньлунь, а также узнав, что случилось с человечеством. Завязка весьма интригующая; пускай во всей этой терминологии было легко поначалу запутаться, разгадывать все тайны было весьма интересно. Сюжет наполнен различными поворотами и драмой. Также мне понравилось, что главный герой "И" не ограничен моральными принципами и расправляется с врагами весьма жестоко.  Уникальная стилистика и визуал сразу зацепили - это технофутуризм вперемешку с азиатской мифологией.  В геймплее тут классическая метроидвания с динамичной боевой системой в духе Sekiro, завязанной на парированиях. Исследование карты, пазлы, взлом терминалов, поиск ресурсов для прокачки и квестовых предметов, а также сражения с рядовыми врагами, мини-боссами (усложнённой вариацией врагов) и уникальными, прописанными сюжетно боссами. Боевка сложная, требовательная к таймингам, не прощающая ошибок, вообще не для новичков. Помимо стандартных ударов, которые наносят мало урона, у протагониста есть талисманы и лук для нанесения уверенных ударов. Также есть система нефритов для усиления различными бонусами героя; всё это можно комбинировать и собрать свой боевой стиль. Со всем этим зачистка карты полностью не становится какой-то проблемой, и я с удовольствием всё собрал и улучшил.  По итогу: уникальный сеттинг, бодрый геймплей, мрачный сюжет, приятная рисовка, а  и саунд-дизайн тоже отменный. Да что я распинаюсь, тут главный герой лечится куря травку, и пополняет максимальный запас здоровья, бухая со скуфом, что еще нужно?)  Однозначно рекомендую тем, кому нравятся метроидвании по типу Hollow Knight.  [h1]8/10[/h1]

Если бы меня спросили, хочу ли я пройти игру снова, то я бы ответил "Так я уже!". Кроме шуток, игра крайне хорошая! Я прошел её на все достижения и сейчас прохожу её третий раз (Первые два забега были для достижений). Скажу сразу, игра весьма сложная, поэтому если хотите насладиться сюжетом и не хотите играть ради платины, то вам сразу в сюжетный режим. А теперь приступим к игре в целом. Сюжет тут построен грамотно, но всегда держит игрока на шаг позади, что многим не нравится из-за непоняток в решениях главного героя (Далее - ГГ). Если вам такое не нравится, то не рекомендую. В этой игре часть лора раскрывается через короткие заметки ГГ, описание некоторых вещей и письма Стражей Сумерек... Ах да, забыл сказать, тут тонны диалогов, которые содержат 80% этого самого лора. Любите читать? Игра для вас. Нет? Ну, ладно, другие насладятся. Ещё пару нюансов. Вы любите жестокость? А вот ГГ любит её сильнее чем бухло и сигареты, которыми он хилится. Простыми словами он готов отрубить ноги и сделать безвольным животным. А кровь любите? Надеюсь, что да, так как её вы будете видеть часто. А паучков? Таких милых созданий вам придется полюбить, так как они тут занимают +- половину экрана, так ещё и появляются под конец игры, так что рефанд вы не сделаете, даже если будете спидранить (В категории No Major Glitches, так как с ними шансы есть, хоть и меньше, чем экранное время Джи в первой половине игры). Что насчет боевой системы, то вам придется подружиться с правой кнопкой мыши или RB, если у вас джойстик. Почему? Увидите. Просто дойдите до первого крупного босса игры. Ты дочитал до этого момента и все ещё хочешь играть? Поздравляю! Игра кайфовая, так что желаю удачи!

Have you ever wanted to play a game that’s kinda like Hollow Knight in exploration, finding important upgrades and big boss fights? What about Ori-styled platforming sections? Oh, what about a big leveling system with lots of skills to unlock in a skill tree? A beautiful art style? Wonderful music? Oh! And don’t forget a story with more text than some JRPGs out there!  Now imagine a game that has all of that and then some.  Sounds amazing, right?  God, I wish.  There is a saying that kinda goes like “too many cooks spoil the broth” or something like that.  Anyway, let’s actually look at some of my issues with the game.  First things first, I have played a lot of platformers and metroidvanias in my time, with my favorites being the original Hollow Knight, Super Metroid and Aria of Sorrow.  In my honest opinion, making a good metroidvania is really difficult. I’ve seen them done badly so many times that at this point I’m pretty sure it is more difficult than most people realize.  Nine Sols starts pretty strong with a beautiful art style and music. Already hitting the right notes. I don’t think anyone in their right mind could look at this game and say that it is ugly. You might not like the art style but that’s honestly pretty subjective.  But pretty quickly you realize that there're some objectively terrible design choices.  My personal biggest problem is that because of the leveling system, a lot of extremely important abilities and parts of the kit, like being able to parry projectiles, are hidden behind the leveling system.  This would not be a problem if XP came pretty easily but out of my 12 hours of play, 6 were just for grinding. You sadly kinda need to start grinding early cause most bosses in the game assume you have your entire basic kit already online and will punish not having access to said kit with stunlocks, insta-kills and sending you back to your bonfire/bench/node.  And I am not talking about the sheer price of everything in the game. If you thought the lantern from Hollow Knight was a bit on the expensive side, then you are going to explode from 4000+ upgrades to healing charges.  Now for the aforementioned parry system.  I love parrying in games. Sekiro is my second favorite souls-like and if the game has a parry system, I love trying to perfect timings and punish opponents.  So when I found out that Nine Sols is a metroidvania with a big focus on parrying, I was rather excited. Parrying is one of the things even Hollow Knight did pretty badly and even Silksong kinda dropped the ball on.  Sadly, when the developers made Nine Sols, they decided to have the parry window extremely short. We are talking shorter than the window Samus has in Metroid Dread to escape the robots. Except now it is for every single enemy, be it a boss, a mini-boss or a common enemy.  You essentially have to be a robot to be able to parry attacks effectively and there is nothing in the game after 12 hours that increases parry windows.  They’ve also decided to punish mistakes by essentially one-shotting the player.  The amount of times I asked myself “why is there even a health bar?” while streaming my sessions has been astounding. Even after increasing your health bar several times, most bosses still one or two shot you.  Healing upgrades? Pointless. Health bar upgrades? Pointless. Any kinda charm/jade that has anything to do with healing? Pointless. Talisman upgrade for healing? Pointless.  So you would think that combat is fast and vicious, low HP on the player and the bosses, right?  Oh you sweet summer child.  No.  Absolutely not.  Your average enemy takes about 15 hits to kill. Elite enemies 30+ on a good day. Bosses? Get ready for multi-phase World of Warcraft style encounters where a boss can take up to 15 minutes but can end you in a single hit.  Or better yet, a boss, tons of respawning goons, all of which can stunlock you at no fault of your own.  Hell they sometimes throw in environmental damage during boss fights that only affects the player, of course.  Seriously, this game has had some of the least satisfying boss fights. Not once have I felt the Dark Souls/Metroidvania “wow that was awesome” I’ve only felt “oh god its finally over I can move on”.  God I miss Mantis Lords.  Now you DO get some tools to fight enemies with.  Like for instance you get a bow that does… some damage, not a lot. And it is limited to a couple arrows. 12 hours in and I was limited to 3 arrows. These 3 arrows did barely noticeable damage to the boss and they can’t even kill your average enemy.  Game thinks the bow is freaking BFG 9000 while it is akin to something like the 9mm pistol from New Vegas.  I’ve also gained a charge attack that’s the only proper means of doing any kind of damage in the game so far. When you get it, you can end average enemies of the first area in a hit or two! It is amazing!  So of course nothing in the leveling system improves it and the damage of it falls off by the time you make it to the Factory/Warehouse area. Which is like the second area of the game.  This area also introduces a constantly appearing whole-screen fire effect that will kill you if you don’t hide in tiny bubbles, that are always difficult to reach and are always protected by either mini-boss class enemies or like 4 enemies, melee and ranged.  All of which ignore this environmental hazard which is sadly normal for this game.  Do you enjoy enemies falling in spikes or having rocks fall on them in Hollow Knight? Well in Nine Sols, enemies don’t interact with the world in any meaningful way.  There’s a lot more here I could complain about, like the sheer amount of bugs I’ve found while playing the game, showing the developers just don’t care and have not tested anything.  Or how the charm/jade system is straight taken from Hollow Knight, except that the half of the jades in the game don’t really do anything.  Like seriously, there is a Quickslash charm from Hollow Knight in this game that lets you attack quicker. Except that in Hollow Knight Quickslash just works, you attack quicker. In Nine Sols, each time you attack IT DOES DAMAGE TO YOU!  WHY?!  And you don’t even attack that much quicker. For that kinda price you would think Yi would attack like twice as quickly or something.  You know, risk and reward?!  The story starts off pretty strong and it even surprised me with the game going from traditional old Asian theme to cyberpunk theme out of nowhere.  The problem is that very quickly you will have characters throwing entire books worth of text at you about stuff that makes no sense.  Like, I love to read. I love CRPGs. Planescape Torment is one of my most favorite games of all time.  But you gotta make your characters interesting. Or at least make the gameplay interesting. Something. Anything. Please.  Anyway, that’s it for the review.  I had high hopes for the game but after the first 2 hours it feels like the developers just gave up on even a small idea of the game maybe being good.  I recommend everyone the original Hollow Knight over this game. Why play a bad copy?

I criticize a lot of modern games for getting the difficulty formula wrong and being un-fun as a result; today I'm going to talk about one that I think gets it [i]right[/i].  I don't have the reflexes of a 20-year-old anymore, so Nine Sols kicked my ass. Repeatedly. Yet there was rarely a single moment when I wasn't having fun. I genuinely enjoyed the gameplay loop of wiping out, trying again, learning all the patterns and tells, and getting a little better each time. The game is unforgiving, in the sense that you cannot tank damage ever, and if your timing is consistently off when fighting any boss (or even miniboss) then you are liable to get ragdolled and comboed into oblivion. But when I messed up, which was often, I knew it was my fault, and I usually knew [i]why[/i].  So yeah, for 95% of gamers, the game is going to be difficult, but it is never cheap difficulty. It doesn't spring obnoxious traps on you, deny you any healing, nerf your abilities or saddle you with debilitating status effects. Controls are absolutely rock solid; attacks are clearly telegraphed, with very distinctive visual indicators for un-parryable moves; parry timing isn't unreasonably strict and more importantly, a badly timed parry does not over-commit you, so it's possible to recover from a broken rhythm; and you pretty much always know whether your timing was perfect, too early, or too late, based on whether you get the satisfying "boing" sound (precise), the "whack" with internal damage (imprecise) or direct damage (whiffed). I'm talking a lot about parries, but, well, it's the core mechanic in this game, so if you're someone who can't stand parry mechanics then don't play this.  I consider it about the same level of difficulty as Silksong or Sekiro (and way more difficult than, say, Ori or Blasphemous), but it's just tons of fun, and it's hard to even put into words how cool it looks and feels when you're eventually able to consistently no-sell the tougher bosses and just... go with the flow. It's like watching a carefully choreographed cutscene, except you're actually playing it. Lady Ethereal in particular was probably one of my favorite encounters of all time in any game, both functionally and aesthetically, though the claw, the Feng twins and the final boss all had their high points too.  I will close by noting that a lot of the story and lore references went completely over my head as a westerner. I'm used to assuming that any words I don't recognize in a video game are probably made-up names, and some of them here appear to be (Tiandao, Tianhuo?). But others are very real references to Taoism or Chinese mythology like Fangshi, Fusang and most of the Sol names, which I only found out [i]after[/i] finishing. I was still able to understand the basic plot, but for any other American apemen out there considering giving it a try, I suggest keeping a Wiki tab open on the side to use as the "codex".

I am hesitating between giving this game a 9/10 or a 9.5/10?  This game is pretty much Hollow Knight with some different mechanics. I liked Hollow Knight so I had a good time with Nine Sols to say the least. The story was interesting enough I guess, not in a bad way ofc I liked it! As well I like the random interactions you can have with the other characters at your base upon findind random items and ♥♥♥♥. Those were neat!  The combat felt fun enough I guess, maybe just because of the parry mechanic that let you block and counter attacks if timed right. As well as what is in your arsenal. Also the health mechanic made me think A LOT of Dark Souls. You have limited heals that you can only replenish at the roots or just the born fires of this game (if we ignore those hum... Pots? Imma call those pots, that refills them and your health). Which kinda makes you try to be more focused during combat so you don't burn all of your heals and die. Which i guess is neat in a way!  The bosses were fine I guess, the Final Boss was kinda cool because of the arena that has like you rotate around what I asume is like a core of some sort? Like that big ball on top of the big root thingy the big tree looking thing. Idk personally it just made the fight look cooler to me.  I don't really have a lot of negatives to give to this game except maybe the travel system that I found just a tiny bit annoying. Because of going to a point A to a Point B you always need to stop at your base in between. I guess it was made so you wont trully miss character interactions? But it's not really that big of a deal just a minor annoyance to me.  I guess I also didn't liked that one stealth section mainly because I suck ASS at stealth. But that's only a big skill issue on my end so I'll let it pass.  In overall I think it was a great game, worth the purchase and my time!

更新补充 年度最佳神作之一 [h2]包含大量剧透 请注意回避[/h2]  [h2]开头[/h2] 科幻世界观的暗黑道教神话的背景，人类道德和原始社会的边界线，科技和道教哲学导致的不平衡，一旦越过了那条线，众生邪恶欲望滋长，科技一旦过于发展，人的思想跟不上就会滥用而导致灭亡 个人剧情这方面评的很高，喜欢这种的世界观和宿命感的类银会非常喜欢，我是很中意这个故事的，没有任何漏洞，很完整很精彩的故事，人物对话不少，基本都能串联但只能在后期知晓重点，一切都会恍然大悟，这种玩家慢慢代入主角经历的自身宿命的纠结感我非常喜欢。  [h2]小感想[/h2] 游戏本身风格是有不少恐怖元素的，中式和赛博科幻恐怖融合，部分场景音乐偏中式阴间调，听久了会有点精神污染，场景中甚至会有Jump Scare，恐怖程度的话看每个玩家的接受程度，我觉得只有戳中会起鸡皮疙瘩，但不会觉得恐怖 BOSS蝶的场景是我认为最诡异也是最喜欢的之一，层层绘制的山海，美好虚幻梦境和残酷现实的交织，地图场景上插着的蜡烛和死人的遗像，横尸哀嚎的血海，空洞流血死在幻境中的人们，阴间的音乐都突出了中式恐怖的感觉，BOSS战的背景夸不来了，得知背景中间那个是蝶的(？)之后真的把诡异值又拉高了一个层次，音画契合，蝶的曲子这种调动人心的作曲真是令人陶醉的，不得不说制作组之前做恐怖游戏的这方面真的有不错的沉浸感  支线不错，人物塑造非常好，很喜欢四季阁里面与轩轩还有神农的互动，每次点休息日夜切换人物都会有作息，很有爱，轩轩你真的六边形战士 主角Yi是见过的古侠道士类最有那种气质的了，理性靠谱有原则又不失温柔，为目标坚定不移的前进（大哥  [h2]关于真结局[/h2] [b]太阳人一族的终结是注定的，这是所有欲望的反噬，像恒说的，他们已经向蓬莱索取了太多 天道好轮回，一个都逃不掉  通常主角在结局因为剧情问题被迫死亡都是一件让人反感的事情，因为大多数都是为了营造悲剧而死，例如拯救世界的英雄情结。但Yi的塑造好就好在他做了这么多错事和经历了这么多的一切依然选择活下去，并不是主动选择愧疚式的赎罪或是死亡 比起直接跟整个新昆仑毁灭，他是抱有着回去见轩轩和大家的想法的，但同时也做好了可能会死的觉悟，一开始他从未想过要以死亡的方式终结，他就像是那种导致了故事中一切的因果，但直到后期之前仍不觉得自己有错，他也许会表达出来，却不会像易公一样因天祸而洗脑安慰自己结果一错再错，虽然前期Yi也是这样固执的性格，但两人的心理动机完全不一样，他能对刚失去父母的妹妹大吼大叫，也能后悔到每一句未说出口的话都去弥补，成百上千次的无限死亡，但却怎么都无法让他迎来真正意义上的死亡 他只会用事实行动去证明他最终会去怎么做，而他确实做到了他能赎罪的最好方式，Yi的角色成长在善与恶之间徘徊，整个人物塑造的非常好 比起玩家认为真结局Yi是选择的自我牺牲，倒不如说他就是被易公杀死的，易公以感染古树的方式强制逼迫Yi终结新昆仑，否则Yi是非常想活下去的，不管怎么说他已经尽力了，死亡也是另一种方式下最好的回归，起码Yi最终与妹妹重逢了[/b]  [h2]美术&音乐[/h2] 每一位太阳BOSS的角色都有参考原型，每位BOSS个性鲜明，人物塑造身材年龄性别出身各有各的特点，音乐都使用了不同的主题乐器，本人本身就是常听音乐的乐迷，战斗音乐乐器使用这方面真的喜欢... 每一首都很喜欢;)   最近一段时间内听的有点上头了 很多首都有人声吟唱太绝了，很浓的中式乐器＋电子使用 BOSS战音乐乐器使用： [b]1.夸伏人声吟唱电音 2.勾芒的铃铛声和太阳旋律 3.奄老的失真电音 4.截全热血的人声和电吉他 5.蚨蝶梦境般撕心唱和钢琴 6.姬神圣史诗的乐器敲击声 7.伏羲女娲的高潮京剧戏腔电音 8.易公进攻般的人声吟唱和宿命节拍与古筝 9.翌的小提琴与高燃的史诗笛子  [/b] 燃（听原声带的时候让人热血沸腾） [b]能在BOSS的曲子里听到类似的太阳人主题旋律，基本上都是类似的调[/b]  较熟悉的太阳主题旋律： [b]夸伏BOSS曲的高潮-姬的高潮部分-女娲伏羲的女声吟唱部分[/b] 还有类似的旋律，每位BOSS都有： 但比较明显的是[b]蚨蝶的曲子钢琴开头-女娲伏羲的女声部分[/b] 还有相似的调： [b]姬的曲子开头-易公的高潮后期 同样的在截全跟奄老[/b]那边也能听到  [b]提一下蚨蝶的区域曲 从开始安静舒适的曲目[i] Peaceful Mind → Path of sorrow → Removing the Facade → Falling Down [/i]最后极大反差的场景变化，极好的诠释了什么是美好的幻境一点点被撕破开来最后真实恐怖的模样 从第二首音乐开始就有类似于扭曲的音符传递，只是一点点逐渐让人感到不安的变化也能让听者意识到，最后一首的彻底暴露，即使是只听四首音乐的人也能通过音乐传达的情绪把脑海里模糊的场景内容和梦境联系到一起，优秀的游戏音乐制作都能很容易的做到这一点，这是我最喜欢的地方之一[/b]  以及能听到类似的重复杂音以及叠加的失真感，感觉就像主角被拉入毁灭的万丈深渊* 但是也是BOSS曲常态了，持续的循环更容易给玩家造成压迫感 游戏美术这方面真的很顶，美术风格真的不错，场景的文化色彩很浓，整个游戏有些地方已经登神了  ED那段万花筒太震撼了，音乐真的居然能做出那种赛博科技和佛教神话营造出那种难以言喻的神圣空灵感配合主角闭着眼在虚空中不停绽放收放的万花筒，不知道为什么ED最打动的我的就是这一块，神性溢出了，一瞬间让我想起经历过的所有剧情和内容，完全沉浸散发着难以言喻的内心情感...仿佛一切都突然被某种不可名状的力量联系起来了，像打通了某条脉搏，让人跟整个游戏的故事世界观包括人物都心连心合起来了，可能这就是某种许久未有的情感吧 [b]脑海里继续响起ED的画面和音乐，画面慢慢下滑的一尊尊代表了各个区域太阳的雕像，音乐是真的能全身心的带动人的精神... 通关后在视频平台回顾了太多遍游戏的美术和音乐，越是回忆情感越是强烈[/b]  [h2]战斗[/h2] 战斗方面个人认为不是入门类银河战士恶魔城作品，游戏偏难，主要在于频繁的格挡攻击和贴符输出，个人贴符贴不好，一直走剑士平A路线，容易跟不上怪被一套连招打飞 但战斗操作流畅打击感强烈，格挡练好了的话很爽，个人认为稳点打能保证最好的体验 前期对新手探路不太友好，主角被击打落地没有无敌帧，容易出现被群伤站不起来的情况 部分地图跳跳乐设计的比较险恶，很多满满的恶意，探索久会有点难受，推荐每一关画面每一关场景都可以慢慢来 需要多训练和多打，但整个游戏的战斗体验依然非常棒，节奏感像在打音游） 后期BOSS的进攻欲望很强，一般模式下难度极高，需要在磨练中不停的背板，刚初见死个十几次掌握BOSS节奏和连招就能打了，战斗负反馈大，就算无伤失误一下也会瞬间被秒，平均来说都要磨一段时间  [h2]关于俩三阶段BOSS的战斗[/h2] [b]比较难受的设计就是即使能熟练的过完一阶段甚至无伤但到了二阶段还是会瞬间被秒，在易公的Boss战里尤其明显，因为她的二三阶段都是初见开头秒杀，这让玩家很难在游玩的过程中去摸索招式 这就导致了1+2+3的长度大大的增加的过关难度，一阶段过了之后二阶段被秒，二阶段好不容易摸清了刚到三阶段又瞬间被秒，即使一二阶段已经能无伤了但你仍然需要花费时间去过完前两个阶段才有机会摸清第三个阶段的出招和操作，我推荐还是提前看攻略，但只能帮助在实战上有一点的招式判断，除此之外依旧非常困难 同时蚨蝶的三阶段战斗里也是同样的，但是容错率比易公高太多，至少不会上来就秒杀[/b]  [h2]体验下来绝对的国产上乘之作，能跟空洞奥日坐一桌 很推荐给玩过空洞&只狼的玩家，这款会很喜欢的[/h2]  [h2]个人在两部都通关后认为 [b]GAME DIFFICULTY＞Sekiro[/b][/h2]  [h2]6.6真结局达成后感想： [b]“生死本是一体，最终我只是回归天地。”[/b][/h2]

Nine Sols was a beautiful game with interesting characters, cool and original story, and boss fights that were brutal, fun and incredibly satisfying. This game is seriously difficult, hard as seven hells. Even though I managed to complete both Hollow Knight and Silksong with true endings, the final boss fight [spoiler]against Eigong[/spoiler] was where I hit my limit, drew the line, and switched to Story Mode so I could finish the game. I can’t say I’m proud of it, especially after beating almost the entire game, but I’ll be turning 41 soon, and my reflexes definitely aren’t as sharp as they used to be :D Even so, I really enjoyed the game, and in my opinion, it absolutely deserves a place somewhere alongside Hollow Knight.

Я не фанат восточной культуры от слова совсем. Аниме я практически не смотрю, мангу не читаю и вообще всё восточное я воспринимаю по чайной ложке в год. Однажды... Я уже забыл когда, правда, но мне случайно попалась эта игра. Кажется, это была осень. Тогда я активно проходил Hollow Knight'ы, и глядя на эту игру, я буквально подумал "Блин, да это китайский Hollow Knight!". Не смотря на то, что меня ничего такое не интересует, я решил таки дать игре шанс. В конце концов её визуальный стиль и схожесть с играми про жуков меня зацепили. Я добавил её в список желаемого, и на предыдущей распродаже купил. К чему это долгое вступление? А к тому, что я сторонний обыватель, который пусть и знаком с играми такого жанра, но не с азиатской культурой, и игра мне очень даже понравилась.    Что же. Первое, что я хотел бы выделить - это колорит. Как на игру не посмотри - сразу ясно, что она китайская.  Это и графика, выполненная в традиционном китайском стиле, пусть и более дополненном. Игра на глаз выглядит очень приятно! А это я ещё не говорю о манга-вставках, которых я ждал на протяжении всей игры, и которые тоже сделаны на совесть! Превосходно, просто шикарно!  Это и музыка, которая способна удивить. Саундтрек сделан тоже достойно. Он довольно разный, но во всех случаях с ноткой востока. Пожалуй, каждый может найти в нём что то своё.   Это и сюжет, наверно? Я опять же не знаком особо ни с чем восточным, чтобы прям уж сравнивать, но основные клише которые даже я знаю тут присутствуют. Коты, милая фиговина встречающаяся на протяжении всей игры, китайские традиционные деревни и города и так далее. Тем не менее, он обширный и даёт перевести дух во время игры. Персонажи прописаны хорошо и многим из них правда сопереживаешь. Со своей задачей справляется очень даже хорошо.  Ну, и геймплейно, наверно? Базируется практически полностью на парировании, и без него будет ой как тяжко. Очень бодрые, но довольно затянутые битвы. Будь они хотя-бы раза в полтора короче, всё было бы прекрасно. Но увы, что есть, то есть. Платформинга тут не очень много, и от него особой сложности можно не ждать, в отличии от битв. Основная сложность битв - в том, что всё базируется на реакции. Одними уворотами тут не сыграешь, а если ты хоть немного отвлёкся - тебя могут очень быстро заставить проходить путь к дао заново.   Итого... Это отличная игра! Большую часть игры я прошёл за эти несколько дней, и в один момент я обнаружил себя играющего в неё 10 часов подряд (при том, что я обычно играю не более 3 часов подряд в одну игру). Я не фанат восточной культуры, но эта игра меня зацепила. Рекомендую.

Amazing game, story and themes can get very dark which i like a lot, game has this somewhat edgier vibe to it compared to Hollow Knight, which is probably the game most people will compare to this one. Differently than Hollow Knight, all power-ups required for progression and exploration are more or less given to you right in the middle of your normal path, so it's basically impossible to miss a power-up needed to progress, which can happen in a lot of metroidvanias. Game also has a mapping system that shows how many items are in each area, so you know when you're still missing something and when you're not, overall this makes the exploration aspect of Nine Sols extremely comfy and straightforward, with little to no backtracking needed. Combat is very fun and very parry-focused, if you don't like parrying, this game is not for you, if you're bad at it, this game is gonna make you learn it. With that said i've found the game considerably easier than Silksong for example, a few bosses are going to give you trouble but nothing more than a few minutes, and after some point dying to normal enemies in the levels becomes very unlikely because you have so many resources to deal with everything.  This is a must-play to anyone that likes Hollow Knight in my opinion. And if you're new to these types of games this is a perfect one to get started because it's not that hard. Pog game.

[h1] Nine Sols : A flawed masterpiece. [/h1] [h2] Quick review (no spoil) : [/h2] I went in Nine Sols completly blind, only knowing that it was a metroidvania. And if you haven't played the game yet, that's definitly what I would recommand you to do. Nine Sols is an amazing combination between Hollow Knight and Sekiro, with a great soundtrack, gorgeous graphism, and a surprisingly incredible story !  For me, Nine Sols is a 10/10 and is honestly not talked about enough. BUT there are a lot of things that makes it imperfect, and troubled me all along my journey. So if you're looking for a comment that will only glaze the game, go away, I will now explain everything that was "bad" about the game, so that better can be made ! So here we go.  [h2] Why did you do that ?... (spoilers for all the game) [/h2] In this first part, I will talk about what I feel like was straight up terrible and I don't understand how it made it in the final product. To begin with, in the last section of the game, after escaping Eigong's trap, we are put in an auto-scroller section, except we have no UI ! Combat with auto-scrolling are already bad enough, but why get rid of the UI ? There isn't anything cinematic happening, and you're just totally confused and annoyed about what's happening ! I don't know if it's made to be harder, or just a glitch, but either way not a good idea at all imo. Now, onto achievements... I'm a 100% guy, so when I started this game, I took a brief look at achievements. Of course, not the secret ones, but in the obvious ones was the one asking to take a card of the Shanhai out of force. So that's what I did... How happy was I when I discovered that by doing so, I locked myself out of two or three achievements, as well as important storyline and all... I can understand the idea, only if you have reason to replay the game. But there is no new game +, no speedrunner achievement, no nothing, and I don't really want to replay the game in it's entirety just so that I can get those achievements... Please game devs, don't do that :( (missable achievements are already annoying enough to now have contridactory ones...)  [h2] Could have been improved... [/h2] I'll be quicker now that I've ranted enough lol. So let's talk about what isn't bad, but meh... The map not having pins to locate stuff (ahhhhhhhh) so simple yet so mandatory in a metroidvania with a map like this one...  There is no invulnerability frames, and it shows. Getting comboed and pretty much one shot after perfect parries is very annoying. In the same vibe, anywhere in the game, ennemies will likely two shot you. Getting more HP just means ennemies deals more damage and you're still two shots away from death... I know, I know, it's a Souls like, but just at some point in the game, give me armor so I can tank a teeny bit better :( Finnally, despite areas being beautiful, I did not feel like I was really transported between areas, they all felt pretty much the same... Caves and lab with sci fi, caves with lake and sci fi, labs with plants and scifi... If you compare to Hollow Knight, (I made pretty much all my points by comparing to HK) the difference is very obvious. And the thing is, we are revealed to be on a [spoiler] spaceship that is our planet [/spoiler] so it would have been really easy to just take any kind of areas, and say that they were just from homeland (which is probably what was done, but then go crazy with it !...)  [h2] Slight complaint [/h2] [h4] Just because I want to [/h4] This part is just me being picky but : I feel like the ennemy diversity could have been a bit more important (always the same style of ennemies no matter how far in the game you are) Difficulty's really weird, you go from being unkillable (like I first tried Goumang and Yanlao) and then the game really started !... Only it had been almost 10 hours and the general before Kuafu was way more trouble (I think goumang and yanlao could have been easily improved in order to be harder, a second phase to Goumang, and a slight upgrade to Yanlao would have been perfect) (other than that, every boss fight is a piece of art ) (except Fuxi and Nuwa, didn't really liked that one don't know why, it annoyed me more than anything) And in this last part, I'll talk about plot and things that didn't click for me.  In the first ending (not the true one, don't know how it's called), Shennong has disappeared from the pavillon and the ultimate weapon given to Kuafu is not mentionned. I fell like just a slight mention could have been nice ?...  And the biggest plot hole imo, how is the pavillon a safe place that can't be found ??? It's litteraly the closest thing from the center who should be a very surveilled area + they went there to turn off Abbacus ! They always know everything he's doing, every movement he's making, but they think it's a safe place ? Nahhh  Well that's it, obviously as I said, I really loved this game, but since every comment is about how it's amazing, I decided to go the other way around ! If you read all that, thanks and have an amazing day ! <3

Отличная метроидвания с добавкой souls, для казуалов(без обид) и детей - не пойдет, очень много завязано на реакции: парирования, увороты, т.к блока нет, хп нельзя овербафнуть, хилок не так много. Сюжет - класс, было очень интересно узнавать истории солов, ГГ, мира, финальный этап наваливает кучу лора объясняющего все.  На геймпаде играть очень удобно. Исследуя все закоулки - 21ч на прохождение до финала. [hr][/hr] [b]Сюжет и допы[/b] - По сюжету это точно не детская история, затрагиваются многие темы личности человека, бесчеловечности, выживания. Мне было ОЧЕНЬ интересно наблюдать именно за доп. историями. Основной сюжет привлекает, но из-за малой осведомленности о мире - часть забывается из-за непонимания.. [b]Мир и исследование[/b] - мир очень большой, с кучей развилок, не все из них доступны сразу. Визуально локации приятные, платформинга много, прыгать интересно. [b]Лут[/b] - валюта, штуки для прокачки, лорные записки. Прокачка[/b] - Качается здоровье, навыки и лук. Опыт на навыки копится достаточно быстро, к финалу у меня остались не открыты 2 навыка по 3 очка. Нефриты(aka пассивки) - много, слотов становится доступного тоже не мало, собрать можно всякое под себя. Вся прокачка подразумевает исследование мира, т.к все спрятано по углам) [b]Бой[/b] - На реакцию, увороты(редко)+парирование(часто), в промежутках бой палкой или "ульта" за очки дающиеся за парирование. [b]Враги[/b] - Разнообразие есть, некоторые враги по мере прохождения изменяются визуально + дополняются новыми комбо атак. Некоторые враги выглядят прям запоминающе, хоть и встречаются редко. [b]Боссы[/b] - Много, некоторые дают прикурить на траев 20-30, атаки у них выразительные, но главное - их истории после победы! [hr][/hr] Но, увы, я завершал игру на ютубе, потому что финального босса во 2 фазе я не осилил)

Nine Sols es uno de los mejores metroidvania que he jugado. Su historia va mucho más allá de derrotar monstruos: Yi no está cazando simples villanos, sino a sus antiguos colegas y líderes de los Solarianos(los 10 sols), el grupo político y científico al que alguna vez perteneció. Tras ser traicionado por quienes consideraba aliados, especialmente por su maestra, emprende una búsqueda de venganza para tomar sus sellos y tomar el control del Consejo.  El combate es simplemente brutal. No depende de esquivas con marcos de invencibilidad, sino de aprender patrones y dominar sus tres tipos de parry: el normal, la patada Taichi y el contraataque ilimitado. Los jefes pueden encadenar combos que al principio parecen injustos, pero cuando aprendes a responder correctamente te sientes como un auténtico maestro marcial.  Difícil, desafiante y extremadamente satisfactorio. Si te gustó Sekiro y los metroidvania, Nine Sols es una experiencia que no deberías perderte.

Você controla Yi, um bicho que é tipo uma raposa que quer DESCER O CACETE nos nove Sols. É uma exploração não linear (mas ao mesmo tempo até que é linear), com upgrades, skills e uma história (e lore) topzera que faz você querer saber mais.  Pra você que é fã de Hollow Knight (e Sekiro?): imagina Hallownest, só que cyberpunk oriental, com chefes que demandam esquiva e parry perfeito em vez de só desviar, e a mesma sensação de descoberta gradual. É BEM DIFÍCIL em alguns momentos, mas recompensador - você vai morrer direto no começo, mas depois que aprender o timing dos ataques, vai se sentir invencível, o fodalhão, o bam-bam-bam... Até chegar no último boss. A arte é topzera, só olhar as imagens lá em cima.  O combate é fluido e viciante. Apesar de comparável ao Hollow Knight, é diferente suficiente, sem ser um clone sem vergonha.  Recomendo DEMAIS, jogue imediatamente, esse jogo devia ser mais famoso.

Despite my Recommend, I have to qualify it as being a "light recommend."  The fighting mechanics are fun, once you learn the parry timing. Any game with a parry system is a good time. But tying your parry into a Riposte-style mechanic with you Qi charges is a great way to force players into using this mechanic, which I think is the game's best feature.  The artsyle is fun, and while I don't know chinese character tropes or design philosophy, I assume these are well done to pay homage to that genre of Wuxia technofantasy.  However, the problems I have with the game (which detract from my overall experience), come from the game being a Metroidvania, but also not fully committing to it. For one, the map isn't really interconnected in the way that Hollow Knight or Ori's maps are. Several areas are dead ends, or lead to another area which is. This isn't a bad thing (Silksong's Mount Fay and Flea Carnival are good examples), but these area aren't optional. Unless I missed portions of the game, I didn't see many optional areas.  The other Metroidvania problem I had is that while the game lets you explore various area, the game is a linear story. I went to the factory area, and was hard locked out of progression because I hadn't killed an unrelated boss on the other side of the map. While something like Silksong is largely linear in its 3 Act structure, there's no cases where you are required to defeat exactly this one boss so you can keep exploring. If this game was more linear, I wouldn't have a problem with this, but because Metroidvanias are fundamentally about exploration and sequence breaking, it's extremely jarring to have to go look at a walkthrough to see the correct order in which to complete the game.  If you've gone through Hollow Knight, Silksong, and Ori, this game is a good, if somewhat underwhelming metroidvania with fun combat. If you're looking to start playing game of that genre, I would recommend another game

Difficult Souls-like with a "Taopunk" setting, unafraid to occasionally get gory and somber.  The boss fights and a few of the setpieces are the real entertainment here.  Your ability to survive will hinge entirely on mashing the trigger and bumper buttons on cue to parry attacks, dash around enemies, and deal bonus damage via a post-attack charge.  Occasionally, I felt the controls were a bit overloaded - I had regularly forgotten which button holds on to railings since my hands are normally crouched between the top of the controller and the attack buttons.  One trick that took a while to learn was patience.  Many of the games enemies benefit from you approaching an enemy with defense in mind before you consider attacking  This ends up giving a "Taoist" rhythm to many enemies where backing off the DPS race is rewarded with more Qi that can be cashed in for larger damage during a single attack.  My understanding is that earlier versions of this game were difficult to navigate.  That was rarely a problem in my playthrough.  There are spots where it's not clear where you need to go so you resort to scanning the map for corners that aren't fully explored.  There needs to be small reminders about why you can't progress in certain areas as occasionally you'll find yourself schlepping to the far corner of the world to only remember that a blank section of map is blank because you don't have the proper ability to progress.  The game length was almost too long.  There's a really weird pacing where every boss is a fun challenge and you find yourself wanting to go back.  Then you'll get a cutscene progressing the story, and find a new area to explore, and then go back to your home base to trade the money you've collected and hang with the friends you made along the journey.  That is all great, but in the time between exploring an area and getting to the next challenging boss, there's a little wear where you feel the game is starting to take too long, and by the 6th or 7th boss I found myself getting burnt out  Despite a few minor gripes, Nine Sols is definitely recommended as an addictive way to practice your reflexes and patience.  The controls are smooth, the checkpoints are plentiful enough, and the writing does keep you invested in every character you come across

Это просто невероятно жестокая игра. Серьёзно, у меня сгорело абсолютно всё, пока я её проходил.  Если вам нравятся Hollow Knight или Silksong, то эту игру я рекомендую однозначно. По ощущениям она максимально близка: и по механикам, и по сложности, и по тому, насколько игра любит проверять ваши нервы на прочность.  Местами это был просто кошмар. Были моменты, когда хотелось разбить геймпад, монитор и вообще всё вокруг. Некоторые боссы и ситуации вызывали просто запредельный уровень боли и раздражения.  Но при этом стоит понимать: игра именно такой и создавалась. Я прекрасно знал, на что иду, поэтому претензий как таковых у меня нет. Это проект для тех, кто любит сложные метроидвании и готов страдать ради удовольствия от победы.  При всём этом игра очень стильная. Она яркая, красивая, с приятным визуалом и атмосферой. А отдельно хочется отметить саундтрек — в конце игры. Музыка реально запоминается.  Итог: если любите Hollow Knight, сложные метроидвании и готовы к тому, что игра будет вас морально уничтожать — Nine Sols вам точно зайдёт.

I wanted to try out some high rated metroidvanias and played this after Hollow Knight. Hollow knight wasnt my cup of tea that I think is overrated, though still a good game. Nine Sols was my cup of tea and I really liked it.  A lot of people that try out this game are Sekiro fans that like the similar combat and Im one of them. Nine Sols delivers on beautiful handmade arts, epic music, good story and a superb satisying combat system. You can say its a sekiro and hollow knight combo in combat and gameplay with cyberpunk theme.   It is a hard game but not that challenging. Hollow knight I would say is a bit harder in combat mostly because of more platform based combat/challenges, Sekiro is definately harder than Nine sols. Except for the last boss that probably is the hardest boss of all time hands down. Last boss took me 5hours 30min. Demon of hatred, Isshin and Consort Radahn all took me around 3 hours ballpark, so they are not even close to this boss difficulty. The boss Lady Ethereal is one of my favorite bosses of all time and I almost wish I never beat her since it was so satisfying fighting her.  The minus is the overwhelming amount of dialogues all the time. Its a lot. You do get used to it and it gets more interesting with progress in time and story.

Venho aqui dedicar alguns minutos do meu tempo para agregar elogios a esse jogo, pois honestamente, o mesmo é merecedor. Um dos melhores metroidvanias que já tive o prazer de desfrutar. Nine Sols conta com um enredo deveras interessante e engajante até o último minuto, propagando aquele vazio comum no final cujo os espectadores sentem depois de apreciar uma boa história. Porém, o jogo não se estende somente a isso. Ouso dizer que o combate(ponto mais destacável do jogo) é o melhor dentre o gênero até onde esse que vos fala, experienciou. O sistema de parry caiu como uma luva para um game como esse. Uma vez que você pega o fluxo, ele te carrega até o final, configurando combates satisfatórios até nos mínimos detalhes. Direção de arte e trilha sonora também pesam tanto quanto o referido acima. Completamente fora desse mundo.  Dito isso, há algumas questões que me deixaram um pouco insatisfeito durante minha jornada no qual, resumidamente, enquadra-se as sessões de plataformas. Algumas fases são mais longas do que deveriam e mal posicionadas se formos levar em consideração o que vem depois. Tirando isso, não tenho muito o que pontuar negativamente... A não ser a espera ociosa por uma sequel ou prequel kkkkkk  Dou um sólido 8 de 10. Sério, joguem Nine Sols!

[i] После боссов здесь мне понадобится отпуск в чём-нибудь простеньком... типа Silksong [/i]  Красочная и драматичная метроидвания о... чём же? Хочется сказать про "добро и зло" или о "роли личности в истории", о "влиянии на будущее", но сюжет здесь глубже, чем у большинства остальных игр такого жанра. Китайская философия, переплетённая с трансгуманизмом и космическим расизмом, и всё это обёрнуто в пробирающий до колен биопанк - такое нечасто встретишь, а по превью на странице в магазине даже не осознаешь.   Даже в геймплее задействована китайская утилитарность. Здесь до конца не оставляет чувство, что это всё неспроста. По сути, в игре просто нет ни одного элемента, от которого можно раз и навсегда отказаться - придётся пользоваться всем, от парирований до луков и "взрывных" талисманов, потому что только так можно выжить и продвинуться дальше. При всём этом, нельзя сказать, чтобы это было затянуто, но боссы действительно дадут вам прикурить - оба Hollow Knight не были для меня таким испытанием, как некоторые из сюжетных боссов здесь.   Потрясает также визуальный ряд из симпатично проработанных фонов и персонажей, где порой находит применение даже псевдо-3D - окружение не вызывает вопросов, не утомляет, а персонажи яркие и разносторонние, что только подчеркивает их личностные качества.  Звуковое сопровождение тоже радует слух - здесь по сути нет озвучки, но при этом звуки, издаваемые персонажами, отлично вписываются в геймдизайн, а электронная музыка в китайском народном стиле создаёт ощущение киберпанка.   В общем, если вы любитель метроидваний, и ищете себе приключение на недельку - крайне советую поиграть в "Девять Солов".

Загалом гра неймовірна, окремо й велечезна подяка за українську локалізацію (це був ключовий фактор через який я придбав гру). Сіт цікавий, історія цікава, побічні квести прикольні, персонажі класні. Біьшість босфайтів радують, доволі цікаві, але не всі. Останні два босфайти особисто для мене стали повним розчаруванням. Якщо в передостанньому ще хоча б музика була прикольною, то битва з Ейґон стала для мене суцільним розчаруванням. Саунд лайняний, механіка бою з нею неймовірно душна та противна. Якщо з попередніми босами відчувався прогрес після кожної смерті, відчувалася якась мотивація та надія накштал "Ось, ось, здається я зрозумів як його/її перемогти, наступний раз буде останнім!", то битва з Ейґон це безперервне отримання стусанів, марні намагання парирувати удари (але вони парируються незграбно, тому користі 0) через пезперервний шквал атак... Можливо моя думка зміниться після остаточного проходження (якщо це трапиться -- я відредагую коментар), але поки фінальний босфайт змушує лише забити на закінчення проходження.

[h1] 難しすぎそうで難しすぎない、ちょっと難しすぎる(？)傑作アクション [/h1]  他レビュー群にて余りにも高難易度を主張されているが、本作はそこまで身構えずにちょっと『構え』る程度で遊べる非常に良質なアクションゲームである。ただし、簡単なゲームだとは口が裂けても言えず、ポップなキャラクターや独特の世界観に魅了される一方で、思わず舌打ちしたくなる歯ごたえと、それを乗り越えたときの達成感という相反する感情を存分に味わわせてくれるだろう。  [h3] ねぇ、またパリィなゲーム？[/h3] このゲームはとにかくパリィなゲームだ。そしてこの情報だけでもう悪寒が走る人も居るだろう。「高難易度でパリィ…」その予想は半分当たっている。  まずは当たっている半分の方からだ。 冗談抜きで戦闘の8割がパリィ前提だ。ボス戦だけでなく、雑魚戦もパリィが無いとまともに始まらない。…まぁ待って、まだ『半分の半分』も伝えていない。更に被弾後の無敵時間もほとんど無く、連続攻撃には連続パリィが必要だし、初撃を被弾しても後続の対処が必須になっている。更に本ストアページにある『パリーで貯めたエネルギー（氣）を敵の体内に送り込み、爆発』させる攻撃が基本メイン火力になるのだが、パリィ『で貯めた』というよりパリィ『でしか基本貯まらない』ため、特にボス戦において回避主体の戦闘は長引き、結果被弾が増えて死にやすくなってしまう。この様に戦闘の根幹にパリィが君臨しており、単なる防御手段ではなく一種のプレイ資格ですらあると言えるだろう。  では、外れている半分は何なのか。実はこのゲームのパリィは他作のものと比べて相当優しい仕様になっている。 今作はパリィタイミングを間違えた際にダメージが回復可能なダメージに変換されるのだ。そして、この回復可能なダメージではHPが0になることは無い。つまり、どんなにパリィが下手でも、パリィさえしていれば生存率は大きく向上する。高難易度によくありがちな『敵の攻撃が対処出来ない→すぐに死んでしまって試行回数が稼げずいつまで経っても上達しない』という負のループに陥らせない設計になっているのだ。また、パリィの受付時間も相当に長いため、最初は失敗を恐れず・ディレイも気にせずにパリィを押すことが鍵となっている。  このゲームはとにかくパリィなゲームだ。今日のゲーマーなら一度は通るであろう、発生の早いあの技に、不自然なディレイのあの技に、ようやく体力を半分まで減らした後半パートに初めて使用してくるあの技にやられ「はぁ…また最初からか…。」のあの体験。ともすれば「はいざんねーんw」とも聞こえてくる様な開発からの悪意に似たものを感じとることもある。今作も間違いなくそれはある。あるのだが、失敗をただの失敗で終わらせない意思も、工夫もそこには確かに存在している。完璧じゃなくても良い、不格好でも良い、だけど諦めないでくれ――今までゲーマーの意地に委ねられてきた『何度も挑戦する』という行為に対し、本作は確かなゲームデザインでもって応えているのだ。  [h3]また台湾が心を曇らせに来た[/h3] 退廃的な世界観をベースにした"タオパンク"と自称するストーリーは圧巻。プレイ後に《返校-Detention-》《還願 Devotion》を開発したスタジオと聞いて納得する程の完成度の高さでプレイヤーの期待に応えてくれるだろう。[strike]映画もだけど、何で台湾って湿り気のある陰鬱作品が上手なの[/strike] ただずっと心が暗くなっていくだけではなく、明るく生きるキャラクターや自らの境遇を受け入れて前に進もうとするキャラクターも存在し、陰鬱な物語に程よい緩急と救いを与えている。[strike]僕は一番右のサムネイルのキャラが大好きです。[/strike]  [h3]まとめ[/h3] 本作は昨今飽和気味だった『メトロヴァニア』、『ソウルライク』、『高難易度』の3銃士を非常に高いクオリティを以て更新した作品であることは間違いない。6年の開発期間を経て満を持して発売されたが、Goodレビューにも大量の苦言を呈されている某傑作の続編よりも発売時期は古いが、こちらの方が新鮮でまた1つ2Dアクションの時代を変えた作品であると筆者は考える。冒頭にも記載したが、他レビューに気圧されて購入を躊躇してしまうのは余りに勿体ない。もしこのレビューで本作に興味を持ったのなら、まずは一度で良いのでパリィボタンを押してみてほしい。

Одна из лучших игр в которую я играл. Только что закрыл на 100% достижений. Игра представляет из себя типичную Метроидванию, но с очень интересной боёвкой и боссами. Дальше я перечислю плюсы и минусы которые заметил лично я. Минусы:  1. Непонятный сюжет и слова в начале, из-за чего может пропасть желание играть. 2. Нужно привыкать к механикам парирования 3. Что лично мне показалось странным, это совмещение китайской мифологии и киберпанка. Плюсы:  1. На самом деле реально очень интересный сюжет с искуплением главного героя. Даже на слёзы пробил. 2. ШИКАРНЕЙНЕЙШИЕ разнообразные боссы. (Битва с Эйгун вообще самая лучшая) 3. Интересное, хоть и грустное взаимодействие с персонажами 4. Фишка, что ты сам должен понимать мир из отрывков диалогов, где мы находимся и зачем всё делаем 5. Разнообразный ландшафт, а с ним и разный геймплей. 6. КОТИКИ!!!  Как итог: Это были лучший потраченный косарь на игру. Огромное спасибо Red Candle Games за незабываемый опыт!
