9/10 - Great story & art design, some really good fun boss fights and a really responsive combat system! I have a few problems with a couple of bosses (Goumang's fight feels awful to play since it's entirely RNG dependant on the two zombies to have favourable attack patterns, and Fuxi's hitboxes are absolutely terrible)  It's more of a linear-progression game rather than a metroidvania, there's a clear path you need to take before you're able to progress to the next area with the bosses coming in a very specific order (with the exception of Ji and Fuxi&Nuwa) but it still can scratch the metroidvania itch.   The Final Boss is soooooo fun, most of the bosses are. Once you learn them and how to counter them, it really becomes like riding a bike whenever you come back. It took me 5 hours to beat the Final Boss for the first time, but now it's therapeutic for me to fight her lol.

Nine Sols - добротна метроїдванія, яка поєднує декілька ігрових жанрів і фішок у щось незвично нове. Можна сказати, що це один із конкурентів Hollow Knight. Навколо цих ігор є багато думок і дискусій щодо того, яка з них краща. На мою думку, Hollow Knight усе ще залишається кращою, але тут є аспекти, які зроблені дуже якісно.  Почну з бази. Геймплей шикарний, керування зручне, візуал у грі неймовірний, а саундтреки - взагалі мед для вух. Механіка парирування додає кайфа до ігрового процесу, причому вона не така караюча, як в інших іграх. Усе грамотно збалансовано, і навіть якщо парирування вийшло не ідеальним, це не викликає фрустрації.  Дуже шкода, що гра розкривається лише десь у середині проходження, через 15–20 годин, коли видають більшість здібностей і механік. На початку ти майже нічого не можеш, і це дратує. Не розумієш, чому немає тих чи інших інструментів, які вже стали стандартом для жанру, і такі думки переслідують тебе десятки годин. Хоча, в принципі, правильно, що механіки вводять поступово й не перевантажують ними відразу, але все одно це може бісити.  Хотілося б ще відзначити, що дослідження іноді здається нудним на початку гри через те, скільки часу може знадобитися для навігації по мапі. Карта - це, мабуть, найбільший мінус гри. Ти ніколи не розумієш, куди тобі йти, коли загубився. Карта може намалюватися сама по собі в місцях, де ти навіть не був, а міток узагалі немає. Через це все, що ти пропустив, ніяк не позначиш і згодом просто забуваєш. Якби додали хоча б мітки, гра вже стала б цікавішою. Мені подобається відчуття, коли отримуєш нову здібність і можеш повернутися в місця, куди раніше не мав доступу, як це було в Hollow Knight. А тут ти просто забуваєш про них, і через це зникає бажання повністю досліджувати карту. У результаті просто пробігаєш усе й навіть не намагаєшся шукати якісь потаємні проходи чи секрети.  Ще пройдуся по мінусах цієї гри. По-перше, це мільярд діалогів. Можливо, комусь таке подобається, але не мені. Їх надто багато. за цю гру я ніби прочитав цілу книжку сторінок на 400. По-друге, це квести, а саме відсутність квестового меню або хоча б якихось нагадувань. Уся інформація про них подається лише в діалогах, які можна випадково пропустити й навіть не зрозуміти, що тобі видали завдання. Через це легко втратити частину контенту. По-третє, це відсутність пресетів для амулетів (білдів). Тут постійно потрібно змінювати свою тактику бою та дослідження, і дуже набридає щоразу переставляти амулети. Це справді могло б покращити гру в рази.  Тепер перейдемо до середини гри, коли вже багато чого відкрито, мувмент повністю розкривається і ти почуваєшся просто божественно. Поговоримо про босів. У грі їх дев'ять, і всі вони пророблені шикарно. Кожен сюжетно обґрунтований, має свою харизму, особистість, унікальний набір атак, візуальний стиль і саундтрек на фоні. Бої відчуваються як справжній танець. Тут зібрані одні з найкращих босів, яких я зустрічав у жанрі, і я гарантую, що ви не пошкодуєте, коли дійдете до них. А широкий спектр білдів допоможе впоратися з будь-яким із них. Під кожного можна знайти свій контрбілд, який, звісно, не принесе вам миттєву перемогу, але точно значно полегшить бій.  Загалом гра чудова. Ті недоліки, які в ній є, повністю перекриваються всіма тими ідеально проробленими аспектами, що затягують у цю гру. Це одна з найкращих метроїдваній, і якщо ви фанат цього жанру або любите складні ігри, які кидають виклик, то Nine Sols точно для вас. Вражає те, що така маловідома компанія створила справжній шедевр, навіть не будучи орієнтованою на цей жанр. Це заслуговує на повагу. З нетерпінням чекатиму на їхню наступну гру.

This game is straight up insane. I know lots of people compare it to Hollow Knight which i think is pretty fair, and in my opinion this game is as good and even better on certain aspects.  **What's worse** : - This is debatable, but i do not find the soundtrack of Nine sols as good as the Hollow knight one. It could be only for me but do prefer the more orchestral approach of Hollow Knight sound tracks  - The ability you get. In Hollow Knight you get lots of ability to chose from and to fight with, such as nail, nail-arts and spells. In Nine sols your combat style is way more limited, you either play the 3 qi charge and spam arrows as they are way to powerful or you play water and spam slash and talisment. Pretty limited in my opinion  - The upgrade. Except the bow upgrade like how do you tell the difference ? I know tao fruit makes you stronger but like i can't see it. I would have like more power spike instead of smooth enhancement which makes you stronger little by little. The power spike your in H.K. are wild to play with, even a nail upgrade is such an improvement, but like spells upgrade, they feels incredible. It would have been cool to get a visual upgrade and feeling upgrade when you enhancement your stuff. AND PUT A FREAKING BOSS FIGHT BEHIND THE TALISMENT UPGRADE, no honestly it would feel way more rewarding than just putting  a skill point in a skill tree  - The mob variety. There are lots of different creatures in Hollow Knight and even if some of the are pretty annoying it is quite great to have that much variety. In Nine sols you will have a grand total of 47 different enemies. Which imo become really repetitive after a little period of time.   - Money. While money isn't necessary to progress like in Hollow Knight, like you need SOOOOO much money to get the arrows upgrade or the healing pipe, like it is insane.    **What's better** : - The Final boss. Absolute Radiance  is a pretty boring fight in my opinion (the most boring is the second phase). I do think that Eigong is better and in almost every way possible. Really proactive fight, no randomness in any means, really satisfying to master and a really good boss fight on how to make you learn about all of your parry possibility   - The jade system. In Hollow knight charm are just broken, they are just way too good, quick slash is absolutely insane along with unbreakable strength, there are no any "down sides" in getting one (except for very specific ones). Nine sols mastered the jade system so nicely. Some jades do have down sides especially those with huge benefits and the most important part, not a single jade feels like a need to have. You are not in a huge disadvantage if you don't have a certain jade. It's just a little ad on, which is very nice  - Map progression. Your first play through in Hollow Knight is filled with "Where the hell do I go now", in nine sols (except for Jiequan), you are guided through the next major location. Well, not directly, they give you hints. The progression just feel a lot more smooth. BUT ! It's is way less opened, in HK you can skip part if you are smart and even for speedrun, by doing semi-entended route but they do not require glitchs. Nine Sols does not give you that much freedom  - The loot can actually be found. Jades and tao fruits aren't hidden behind almost invisible breakable walls which is insanely better if you want to 100% the game.  - Death is better. Death is less punishing and frustrating.  - Bosses. Even if there are fewer bosses they are really high quality, like a bad boss in Hollow knight is  Markoth, frustrating, not innovative, not really fun, not lore related etc...  A bad boss in Nine sols, well, i don't even know i liked them all but some people say that Goumang is the worst, although it is innovative, fun imo, has really cool lore, is not that frustrating, etc...

There's a baseline that Metroidvania's have to meet - one that many others have met: unique art style, fluid mechanics, and interwoven level design that rewards exploration and provides consistent progression.  This easily meets all of those requirements, but it's rich story/lore and incredibly strong themes put it a cut above almost all of them. If you're a fan of the genre, don't skip this.  1. There's a standard ending, and a "True" ending. To achieve the true ending, THERE ARE SOME REQUIRED MISSABLES. LOOK IT UP. The ending is worth the effort, trust me. 2. There are some "Souls-like" elements to the combat (parries/deaths make you lose your currency), but there is a "Story" mode that greatly nerfs the difficulty. It doesn't lock you out of any endings, just an achievement for finishing the game on the "Standard" mode.

I played Hollow Knight on my Nintendo Switch in 2018/2019. Didn't even know that it is a metroidvania, couldn't care less to be honest. I finished that game with 30 hours of pure enjoyment and raved about it to my friends and became a game synonymous with the Switch, to me at least. That experience of lying on my bed and playing it before sleep, brings memories of the Game Boy. This game does lean somewhat to what Hollow Knight tried to do, and did so phenomenally well. But honestly, I really didn't see Hollow Knight in this game that much. This is its own thing. Do not try to find a Hollow Knight clone in this game. Actually, I played Nine Sols cause it was recommended as a good souls-like with its difficulty--which yeah this game is difficult--and so I played it on that ground. I will tell you that playing this game, I didn't even feel time pass. Hours felt like mere minutes. If you want a souls-like experience in a metroidvania, this is the game for you!  Story:  I would best explain this story as revenge story with more depth, themes explored, and characters variety than your run-of-the-mill revenge story. The cast is phenomenal with so many characters with depth and background that you feel for every one of them and empathize with them all, even those who the main character wants to take revenge on. There are twists and turns and you can see the main character changes and molds to, at the end, a version you did not think he would reach. Even the themes that the writers are exploring are diverse and many. From science to spiritualism, to emotions and ideals that we carry are explored well and provide a fantastic backbone for the world and the characters. I like that the Nine Sols are well differentiated that encountering them feels different and is not repetitive.   Combat:  Parry, parry, parry! The game is heavy on the parry. In fact, it is its most important feature. I wouldn't say that it should be mastered like the parry system in Sekiro--though that was a very good parry system--but it is easy to master. What is intriguing is the type of parries that you have. Different attacks require different parrying techniques you would learn throughout the game, in addition of a dash that would deepen the combat and add an interesting complexity, especially adding to the difficulty of the game.  The enemies are diverse enough and gradually demand more skill to deal with them, keeping you engaged till the end. And the bosses? The icing on the cake! Hands down phenomenal boss encounters. The mini bosses honestly feel somewhat forgettable, not bad or boring, but nothing stands out. But the main bosses are cool, demand technical mastery of the combat system, and deliver a good chunk of the game experience along with their respective stories.   Environment and sound design:  The game has a good environment and aesthetics. Maybe I got a little bored with the techy environment, but overall, the environment is great. Sound design is a step over the environment. Steps, clashes, hits, parrying, all of them sound so good and so clean. I actually was reminded of Sekiro due to how good the parry sound and that game has much more budget and audience than Nine Sols. That should tell you something of the quality of the sound design.   Music: I wasn't awed by the music of this game. It is not bad per se, but it didn't really stick with me, only like one song at the credit that I returned to listen to, which is my criterion of a great video game music. It does fit the atmosphere, but nothing to pull me to listen to those themes.   Performance and issues: 180 FPS capped and stable! Only handful of times I saw some stuttering, which immediately went away, so I do not think the game performs awfully, way far from that.  On the other hand, I swear the hit boxes of this game are sometimes off. This is not to say I do not make mistakes in-game, but sometimes I swear that: "Man how! I did press the button!" It feels sometimes that the game just didn't get the input from me or the hit boxes are way bigger than what they should be. You encounter those weird instances. Although, obviously not many, the game is well-polished. But frustrating for sure.  Summary: This is a fantastic game with amazing story and lore, addictive combat, and a world begs to be explored. This game is difficult and might have some of you to quit. But it wouldn't be a s good as it is without this difficulty. It is a rollercoaster of emotions and enjoyment that it will leave its imprint on you. Absolutely recommend this game. Can't wait for Red Candle next game!

I have more hours than it appears due to originally playing a free copy. Unless you're god tier or use the built-in easy mode, most of the bosses will become a wall you need to overcome.  Even though I'm garbage at it, I definitely recommend Nine Sols. It's a solid game. However, keep in mind that this mostly about the bosses. If you're playing this because you enjoy metroidvanias, you might not gel with it that much. Incredible game! Very recommended for those who loved Hollow Knight/Silk Song and Sekiro as it blends both in a seamless way. The story is emotional, profound and philosophical.  The combat is rhythm-based when the parrying system does most of of the heavy lifting - it is incredibly rewarding to perfectly parry a sequence of attacks to gather your Qi, and then blast your enemy with focused Qi attacks to deal huge chunks of damage - the developer gets it. The art direction is incredible - every single area has a distinct look to it, with different themes to each one that by structure alone tell a story behind the area. For example, the 1st area, the apeman facility, has a shock value to it that I will not spoil. Each zone has a unique vibe to it. The only thing I found kind of lacking is enemy variety compared to other incredible games from the same genre such as hollow knight, but that is almost unfair, as there are about 30-40 different enemies in Nine Sols.  The music is incredible as well, with each zone having different music that represents the vibes of each zone with confidence.  The character design is inspired, and the NPC's are very fun to interact with - both the bosses (which I will get to) and the friendly companions that join your hub. I was initially afraid that Shaunshaun will turn out annoying or uninteresting, but I was happy to see how much his growth matters to the game, and how giving him different trinkets and artisan tools allowed not only him to grow, but also for the hub area to grow. It kind of feels like your own child is growing as you raise him to become something incredible, and it's heartwarming to see its effect on both us, the players, and Yi - the protagonist.  As for the bosses - it gets better the more you play. Out of the main antagonists, there is only one which I felt like fighting them was tedious. It truly feels like you get enemies that get tougher and tougher the more you play this game, and the bosses, generally speaking, also adhere to this method. Each boss feels unique with an interesting gimmick that polishes their basic abilities and allows them to truly shine, while each boss has a setup that feels really well designed and thought through very thoroughly.   I think the only thing i'm upset about is that the more you play, the better the game becomes, and when you reach the final boss, you kind of don't want to end - you just want to fight more unique bosses and to continue experiencing exhilarating combat with difficult adversaries.  Overall, I would say this is between a 9-10/10 game for me if I had to rank it. It is slightly slow until the 2nd main boss, but the more you play the more it clicks. I cannot recommend this game enough. I hope you try it and enjoy it as I did :) Also hell yeah there's a boss rush mode that becomes available after finishing the game!!

The dev team made psychological horror games before this and dear god it shows.)

After 26 hours, I can confidently say that this game is very challenging, even on Story Mode. The world is beautiful, yet filled with dread and violence, creating a unique atmosphere that constantly keeps you engaged. The soundtrack is mystical and perfectly complements the game's setting and mood.  This is easily one of the best stories I've experienced so far in a metroidvania. It's intriguing from start to finish, with several great plot twists and a rich lore that kept me wanting to learn more. Yi is an excellent protagonist whose character development shines throughout the game, especially as his quest for vengeance unfolds.  The platforming can feel tricky at first, but once you get used to the mechanics, exploring New Kunlun biomes becomes very satisfying.  The map could be more user friendly but the game have a mechanic that allow you to backtrack a bit easier mid progress. Combat is engaging and demanding, with numerous parry variations that require practice and precision. It can be frustrating how the game test your patience at times, but the challenge is rewarding and fun.  The boss fights are where the game truly shines. They are tough and require you to learn each boss's patterns, knowing exactly when to parry, dodge, and attack. Every victory feels earned.  If you're looking for a metroidvania with a compelling story, fantastic world-building, very challenging combat, and memorable boss battles, this game is absolutely worth playing.

[h3]Достаточно долго откладывал прохождение этой игры и в итоге очень пожалел о своём решении.[/h3] [b]Игра потрясающая![/b] Визуал шикарный, музыкальное сопровождение на высоте, а боевая система действительно увлекает. Единственное, чего мне немного не хватило, — это челленджа. Вся игра была пройдена практически на одном дыхании, и серьёзных проблем с боссами, мини-боссами или рядовыми противниками у меня не возникало (за исключением финального босса). Но тут всё индивидуально: то, что показалось лёгким мне, для кого-то может оказаться настоящим испытанием, и наоборот. Отдельно понравилось исследовать локации и читать описания предметов. Лор в игре довольно интересный, а местами даже забавный. В общем и целом игре ставлю однозначный лайк и смело рекомендую ознакомиться с ней всем любителям жанра.

Fantastic little Sekiro-like. The metroidvania and exploration elements aren't the selling point here - the real stars of the show are the storytelling, a wonderful Taopunk narrative, and the boss fights, which ask you to learn a challenging dance of different parries and dodges.   Really enjoyed every one of the ~20 hours I spent with it.  Note that the challenge on the last few bosses is quite a step up from the rest of the game. The final boss took me around 70 attempts, though its a testament to the game's design that once I had mastered a phase of the fight I was able to blitz through each phase's health bar in a matter of seconds to try the next phase.

A superb metroidvania that draws inspiration from Sekiro and Hollow Knight while forging it's own unique identify that is fantastic. Be warned that it is a an exacting game that demands mastery of its systems to overcome, but there is a story mode for those who are not gaming masochists.

This review is written by a gamer who is bad at video games (I never managed to finish New Super Mario Bros Wii, for example...) and for whom Nine Sols is the first game played of this genre. If this is relatable, then this review is for you! Well if you're good at videos games, you can also read it :3  So this game is HARD, but because I'm a tryhard who reaaallyyyy wanted the achievement you get for finishing standard mode, I pushed through, despite wanting to rage quit multiple times. But it is honestly brutal. Very very brutal. If you have skill issues like me, you WILL struggle. Most of the hours of my playthrough are of me struggling to beat the bosses. But, honestly? The game is just too wonderful to quit.  Not only are the story and the art well-crafted and beautiful (who wouldn't want to play as a cute little cat-like creature doing martial arts?) but the map exploration is also really fun. I liked the different ways to move around, getting little treasures... Even the fight mechanics are fun! I swear! I'm not used to parrying, so it definitely has a learning curve, but I don't regret any of it. As someone who speaks Chinese, I was also so happy to see it integrated in little parts of the game! The taopunk universe is so cool!!  I can't wait to boot up the game again to replay it and get the last achievements I'm missing. Nine Sols is a beautiful work of art which totally earns its spot in my top 5 favorite games ever. I need to bug all my friends to play it now!  So happy that I gave this game a chance, and if you're hesitating still, please try it out <3 I'm sure you'll enjoy it~  (Oh and I don't have a very high performance laptop, but still managed to play smoothly! Also love the red-green colorblind accessibility feature being a thing!)

В целом битвы с боссами напоминали изучение музыкальных композиций (как бы это странно не звучало), потому что в кульминации ты начинаешь парировать атаки "на слух" в определенном темпе.  Из плюсов: - Классный подход к механикам геймплея; - В меру сложная и разнообразная боёвка (здесь не прощаются пропуски атак и ты не мигаешь неуязвимостью после получения урона, надо прям заучивать паттерны на парирование); - Шуаньшуань; - Мне либо повезло, либо гринд в этой игре действительно не душный и мне под конец хватило ресурсов на открытие всего для ачивок; - Музыка, но только местами (хотелось бы побольше музыки а-ля Хотлайн майами, как при прохождении складов и побега из лаборатории).  Из минусов: - После такой истории хэппиэнд был бы в тему... - Недостаточное развитие линии с Хэн (мне показалось, что эту ветвь повествования просто слили).

先说结论，在以本土神话为基础再创造的剧情设定加持下，本游戏可跻身类银游戏第一流。 本游戏首先以动作战斗为长，一大战斗特色在于通过格挡无效化敌人攻击，在特殊条件下甚至可以给敌人反弹内伤，在经由另一种特色功能“符箓”引爆为直伤，从而做到防守即是进攻——而这机制在后期仍有进一步延伸。如果能适应本游戏的这两大特色战斗系统，再基于本作敌人旺盛的进攻欲望设计，那么就能收获不同其他游戏的绝佳战斗体验和爽感（部分的boss设计也在特色战斗系统的基础上显得相当出色，最终boss的设计甚至可入类银顶级之列）——触发精准格挡时的锣声令人身心愉悦。不过在设计的另一面，主角受伤后的无敌帧极短，且不做任何操作的情况下有着极长的受伤后硬直，会导致不留神的情况下容易被一套秒杀。 本作的另一长板是设定与剧情。游戏对本土神话作出了相当出色的科幻向改编，故事中绝大多数的设定和命名都有考究和贴合之处，很适合中国宝宝的体质。游戏的故事内核其实和另一款类银佳作《mio》有异曲同工之妙，但本作还有着极其突出的角色塑造，尤其是主角——在众多类银游戏中显得立体、生动，和真结局后实现的圆满。围绕主角展开的配角塑造也不乏精彩，主角在特定场景和时间节点同特殊配角的互动，以及部分场景的变动，都很是细节用心。两者结合，共同编织出了一个逻辑自洽（很高兴制作组从一开始就清楚得知道自己要写什么样的故事）、令人动容的优秀悲剧。 本游戏称得上缺憾的地方，大概是作为类银的流程过于线性，大地图与大地图之间的连接也乏善可陈，缺乏能力锁解开后的探索畅快感，也没啥有难度的跳跳乐（这点见仁见智），所幸地图内部的探索体验还算不错。 本游戏的美术风格也算具备特色，部分场景设计“ 令 人 惊 喜 ”（笑），音乐方面点名表扬初期猿人监控站的bgm，起到了很好的奠定游戏基调和渲染氛围的效果，部分boss战bgm搭配对应boss的鲜明特色，也能调动玩家情绪。 评分：8.9/10

整體設計想法不錯，在沒看攻略的情況下通過一般難度。 後期劇情差強人意，並沒有給我任何意料之外的劇情。 玩法新穎但說實話並沒有設計到最好，弓箭說實話沒什麼必要切換成另外兩個型態，對於一些人來說整個戰鬥比較是在練肌肉記憶，玩法比較單一。 姬那邊的劇情跟戰鬥是我覺得非常可惜的地方，後來看到別人說因為趕行程所以就只能變成現在這樣。 不知道是不是趕工的問題，相比前面，後半部感覺是半成品，並沒有仔細地打磨。 了解開發商的難處，但後期半成品給我的感覺，反而讓我覺得時間整個浪費掉。 整體給個８分，不推薦一般玩家休閒玩，建議上網看個劇情就好。

就游戏本身而言， 优点：美术和音乐都可圈可点，赛博道教这块；学会弹反后大多数boss都打得很爽，boss基本上都逃不了课，有种见招拆招的感觉，尤其易公。 缺点：地图比较平庸，基本上没有探索的欲望，道教石窟那里各种传送门，然后地图还不能标记，无敌了只能说；强推弹反，虽然弹反学会了确实很爽，但是打boss的时候基本都只能弹反；没有无敌帧，很容易丝滑连招死掉，尤其是前期跑图的时候，因为这个掉了不少金，又没有像丝之歌一样搓珠子之类的来防止掉钱，导致血压非常高；玉石系统基本上没有用，只有少数几个玉有点用，前期没槽基本上装不了啥玉石，比较鸡肋。

Nine Sols is a breathtaking game with a great atmosphere, hard but fun gameplay, and a very engaging story. Despite the game's punishing difficulty, I never gave up on completing it because going into it you will begin to care a lot about the world and its characters. This game tells its story wonderfully through both environmental storytelling and emotional character moments. The chinese aesthetic is really the cherry on top for me, Having grown up being fond of chinese architecture and cultural history. And the soundtrack. Oh my god, the soundtrack. Props to all the amazing people who worked on this game. It's still hard as hell tho, haha, so be prepared.

처음에 그렇게 큰 기대를 하지 않고 시작했는데 진짜 재밌게 플레이한 메트로배니아 겜이였음 개인적으로 다른 메트로배니아보다 이 겜을 먼저 해보는 걸 추천해보고 싶은 정도  게임성으로는 일단 가장 좋았던 점은 메트로배니아임에도 진행이 꽤나 매끄러움 다른 여타 같은 장르의 다른 게임들에서는 그냥 오픈월드에 플레이어를 던져놓고 탐험시키는 방식이 주였다면, 이 겜은 npc나 퀘스트(뭐 띄워주거나 하진 않지만)를 통해 다음은 어디를 가야할지 제시해주는 점(ex. 여기를 가보는게 어떠신가요?/동쪽으로 가보는게 어떨까요?)이 하면서 맵을 너무 헤매지 않고 매끄럽게 탐험하게 도와줌 물론 맵 싹싹김치 하고 싶은 사람들은 예전에 가봤던 곳 다시 가보긴 해야함 이건 뭐 메트로배니아 특이니 넘어가고  거기에 전투 시스템은 만족감이 매우 좋음 패링의 활용을 극단적으로 권장하는 방식이 누군가에겐 독이 될 수 있으나 점점 패턴을 익히고 완벽하게 패링한 후 클리어하는 경험은 도파민이 뿜뿜함 진짜 재밌음 [spoiler]개인적으로 막보스 역공은 진짜 개재밌었음 패링과 턴잡기의 극한을 맛보여준 기분 얘가 막보인게 아쉬울 정도 나중에 dlc든 2든 출시해서 이런 빠르고 어려운 난이도의 보스들을 더 보여줬으면 좋겠음[/spoiler] 그렇다고 반드시 패링으로만 잡을 필요도 없고 다른 세팅이나 도구들이 다양해서 자신만의 빌드와 방식으로 몹들을 공략할 수 있는게 좋았음  그렇다고 스토리나 아트, 사운드가 하자가 있냐? 그것도 아님 등장인물들도 각자 개성이 있게 디자인 좋고 맵마다 특색도 확실해서 좋음 동양풍 sf적인 세계관과 그에 맞는 음악과 효과음도 매력적으로 잘 그려내서 인상적이었음 스토리적인 부분은 내 입장에선 10% 정도 빼곤 다 납득되고 이해가 가능하게 흘러가서 만족스러움  살까 고민하는 분들에게 꼭 사라고 강추하고 싶은 게임 앞으로 이런 동양풍의 게임들 많이 나왔으면 좋겠다

Отзыв к "Nine Sols" от Игрока Казуала... охх, я не знаю с чего начинать, это так очень волнительно для меня что дрожу, хорошо оставлю свой пару мнений которые мне пришли. P.S: Я не ожидал что очень много инфу напишу и лишней воды.  [h1]"Геймплей 9/10"[/h1] Я терпел эту игру учился парировать когда я первый раз играл, я не понимал значение боевой система "Sekiro" и парирование (я ни разу не играл Sekiro), но несмотря что проигрывал много раз в бою с боссами и неожиданными врагами, это меня больше тянуло чем отталкивало. (да я не буду скрывать одну вещь, что у меня часто бомбило и испытывал стресс, Вот моя клавиатура ноутбук был в целом состояний)  Я понял что уклонение и паника они бесполезное, вместо агрессивной игры надо улучшать реакцию и скорость навыка успевать парировать, таким образом тебя не могут добить когда ты совершаешь идеальные парирование и маневрировать доминируя. (Но одна ошибка может сильно укусить за твой зад)  Когда часто умирал, я скорее обвинял себя а не игру и его хит-боксов, ловить тайминги правда сложнее и есть неожиданные вещи, меня раздражало что босс имеет дополнительные фазы без предупреждения но потом прошёл. Во временем было интересно изучать способности и разблокировки Навыков, также как работают каждый полученные "Нефриты", получить их всех для меня казался сложным чем Hollow Knight, Амулеты легко находились и покупал спокойно в моем прошлом.  [h1]"Сравнение двух игр"[/h1] Сравнить геймплей "Рыцаря с HK" vs "Йи с Nine Sols" это так трудно и вызывает спор для разных лиц, но каждый выбирает по своему вкусу, Йи для меня выглядит Хорошим но я выбрал бы Рыцаря из-за лёгкости. Потому что я не привык полностью к Ритмичным и Дуэльным боям к сожалению, ранее я игру больше играл только Hollow Knight что привык к нему.  Игру словами не могу описать, Жанр "Метроидвания + Даопанк" вышло очень офигенно! Я к "Метроидвания" очень давно привык благодаря "Hollow Knight", с помощью него я успеваю привыкать к 2Д миру и исследованию, но все жё я мог потеряться в некоторых этапах и не понимать как пройти дальше. Баланс у обоих одинаковый, насчёт сложности я отдал бы к обоим но есть различий, Hollow Knight Выигрывает за большой длинный контент из-за Режима "Стальная душа (заставляет начать всё с нуля) и 112%", а Nine Sols Выигрывает за Уникальный бой и Необычной Экшеном/Механикой. А передача эмоций и диалоги балл уходит к Nine Sols, Hollow knight тоже отличен по эмоций но она скрытно её передает что в нём много страданий и мрачного.  Двое получают баллы за "Атмосферу, Исследования, Глубокий Лор"  [h1]"Атмосфера и Сюжет 10/10"[/h1] Я не знаю что сказать насчёт "Даопанк" что это смесь "Киберпанк и Китайской мифологий", но вышло явно красиво несмотря что встречал жуткий реализм, анимация и дизайн персонажей/локаций я их ставлю на божественном уровне. к Музыке я относился нейтрально и оно казался обычным фоном игры, но углубляясь интересуясь игрой музыка медленно начал нравится в некоторых последних локациях. Момент когда я прошел игру и услышал "Collage", я больше обожал инструментал и HK Саундтреки, но это песню считаю отличной в оценках что она отражала трудный путь Йи его борьбы и утраты.  [h1]"Персонажи 10/10"[/h1] Главный герой Йи довольно отличный, но я советовал бы всем не думать сразу Протагонистов что они правда герой не зная скрытое и кем они являются самом деле. НПС, Союзники, Антагонисты тоже довольно отличны, хотя бывает некоторых странных, они позволяют тебе испытывать множеств эмоций к ним и у них предысторий 8/10, я ненавидел больше всего [spoiler]"Цзецюань, Эйгун, Яньлао".[/spoiler] Некоторым испытывал жалость [spoiler]"Леди.Э"[/spoiler] а иногда их очень трудно оправдать [spoiler]"Гоуманг, Фэнги".[/spoiler] А персонаж который заставлял больше всего меня сопереживать это "Йи и его родственная [spoiler]младшая сестра Хэн", я чуть не плакал читая эти записи.[/spoiler]  [h1]"Техническое состояние 10/10"[/h1] Играл на 720p и высоком качестве, фпс давался довольно хорошо 40-55 fps, хорошо Играбельно на встроенной видеокарте и малой оперативной памяти AMD, встречал странных моментов проблемы с кадрами в локаций Павильон но со временем она сама ушла, не встречал фризы и замерзаний кадров и бой проходился спокойно, спецэффекты и хаос не мешали сильно, я ни разу не сыграл на 1080p но думаю фпс упадёт до 30-40 фпс. Баги в игре не встречал ни разу.  [h1]"Цена и Контент 9/10"[/h1] Купил игру на 2,5k Тенге несмотря на региональные ограничений, не жалею что купил Nine Sols, я очень рад что компания устроила вторую годовщину и сделала скидку, а то давно думал купить Nine Sols и ждал хорошей цены...  [h1]"Итоги"[/h1]  Я казуал который ни разу не играл Sekiro. Я умирал сотни раз.  Я бомбил. Я злился. Я страдал.  И всё равно прошёл до конца.  Это и есть Nine Sols. Игра которая верит в тебя больше чем ты сам,  и в итоге оказывается права.  Также я был удивлён что игра от небольшой тайваньской студии, но игра вышла на уровне "Absolute Cinema". [b]9.5/10 — шедевр который я не забуду.[/b]  [i]P.S. Клавиатура выжила. Еле-еле.[/i]

[h1] Sufrimiento taoísta en su máxima expresión [/h1]  Inicialmente decidí jugarlo por todas las recomendaciones que veía por ahí, así que terminé dándole una oportunidad… y aunque entiendo perfectamente por qué a tanta gente le encantó, conmigo la historia simplemente no terminó de dar ese feeling.  [h2] Historia: empezó floja y terminó “bien”… pero tarde [/h2]  La trama inicia con una vibra de venganza bastante interesante, pero poco a poco va girando hacia una redención que, honestamente, sentí que llegó demasiado tarde.  No diría que la historia es mala, simplemente nunca me logró “tramar” del todo.  [h2] Lo mejor: gameplay y dirección artística [/h2]  Eso sí, donde el juego realmente brilla es en la jugabilidad y el apartado artístico. El combate es MUY satisfactorio cuando le coges el ritmo, y el estilo visual tiene muchísimo cariño encima. Honestamente, me habría gustado ver más escenas estilo cómic porque se nota demasiado la dedicación que le metieron.  [h2] Consejo para nuevos jugadores [/h2]  El [b]Parry[/b] es tu mejor amigo. No, en serio. Aprende a usarlo o prepárate para sufrir.  Este juego es perfectamente apto para masoquistas: vas a morir muchísimas veces, incluso contra enemigos random que ni siquiera son mini-bosses. Pero cuando finalmente dominas el combate, se siente increíble.  [hr][/hr]  [b]Puntuación personal:[/b] 7/10

Негативный отзыв оставлять не хочется, но рекомендовать игру точно не буду никому. Красивая картинка, глубокий лор, - но на этом, пожалуй, все... Я не знаю почему в 24-25 годах в играх возводили в абсолют механику парирования, хотя, на мой вкус. это самая скучная механика, которая есть. В игре нам дают прокачку, дают доп.оружие в виде лука, дают даже возможность качать и увеличивать хп. НО! Нафига тебе это все не нужно, ведь тебе нужно просто выучить тайминги парирования, парирования в прыжке и усиленного парирования... Талисманы тоже как будто бы не дают вариативности билдостроения, как например в полом рыцаре. Лично я нашла две более менее годные сборки: одна на увеличение урона, вторая на увеличение терпильности. Игра предлагает тебе из сложностей либо прогулку для детей, либо портал в ад. Про жизни персонажа, кстати, это первая игра, где у меня при полной прокачке была полоска жизней длиннее чем в дарк солсе, но она мне не помогла, ведь босы выносят тебя с 2-3 ударов в стандартном режиме игры.  Хочется похвалить нарратив в игре, история действительно увлекает, если читать все записки и журнал. Сюжет про серую мораль, кровожадность и муки выбора. Правда, из-за того, что там переплетаются тайм лайны, в повествовании можно немного запутаться. Поэтому рекомендую посмотреть просто видосы о лоре игры на youtube.  Уровень с тюрьмой я вообще комментировать не хочу, это просто ****. По мне это очень слабая метроидвания и дизайн уровней очень простой.  В общем игру конечно я прошла, но впечатления о ней больше негативные.

¿Cómo lo ha hecho?  En serio, soy alguien que ODIA el sistemas de "Parry", probablemente nunca juegue al Shekiro por tener este sistema de juego como núcleo (entre otros factores que me crispan o no me gustan). Pero este juego, lo ha logrado, no solo me ha gustado el juego, los gráficos son más que preciosos cuya la animación me atrae y agrada por igual. La música es una verdadera delicia y la historia, madre mía, bebe de los "soulslike", sí, pero te cuenta una historia clara y si quieres averiguar el trasfondo, debes de encontrar los archivos de memoria y prestar atención al entorno. No me es raro que digan que es el matrimonio de Sekiro y de Hollow Kight, porque es una descripción muy acertada. Y bueno, no me es un juego perfecto, me ha encantado, desde luego, pero tiene fallos, el primero es que la música no se oye mucho, que sí, te dicen que pongas cascos, pero vamos, no me fastidies. Quiero disfrutar del juego con esa tremenda banda sonora de buenas, no tener que configurarla yo o tener que ponerme los dichosos cascos. Y la segunda, aunque es personal mío y a raíz de mi disgusto de mecánica, es juego puedes pasártelo en gran medida sin hacer el parry... hasta que llegas el jefe final... Mal rayo parta a esa bruja.  Y bueno, por lo demás, maravilloso, uno de los mejores "Metroidvenias" del mercado, y no es precisamente caro a diferencia de otros juegos que salen incompletos. Y se le nota que esta cargado de amor.  He escrito esta reseña al pasarme el juego, y el tiempo dirá si conseguiré el final verdadero... porque me he informado como obtenerlo... lo difícil no es los pasos, el problema es el combate final... que es más complicado... Adiós... xD  Hola, esta es mi adición  ala reseña al conseguir el True Ending... ¿Ha merecido la pena? ¡Hasta al ultimo maldito segundo de mi existencia! He sudado, me he frustrado, he maldecido a dioses que no creo... pero ese momento de vencer y tener un final memorable que te dice "Sí, eso que sientes es felicidad porque no te has rendido y lo has conseguido". Pocos juegos han conseguido hacerme sentirme así.  Este juego es hermoso en todos los aspectos, por favor, dadle una oportunidad, se la merece de verdad. Aunque sea para decir que no os gusta... Pero tengo la sensación, de lo que haréis lo mismo que yo, lo daréis todo por conseguir el verdadero final...  Eso si, eso de logros perdibles que se consiguen a largas horas cerca del final... maldita sea, en serio, maldita sea... xD

Топовая игра. Во первых - не холлоу найт, ближе к секиро, что и логично. Во вторых, очень понравилось, как разработчики умудрились расставить грамотно все ключевые события. Игра не ощущается как бесконечная беготня от босса к боссу, локация за локацией, ибо здесь периодически во время сюжета происходят разные, интересные события Хочу выделить благодарность как раз таки за павильон. Банально приятно часто, после возврата ходить и слушать нпс, участвовать в каких то сценках, которые влияют на сюжет. Вспомнить к примеру когда мы деревню отбивали, такое мне очень понравилось.  Теперь по поводу боссов, их здесь относительно мало, и больше половины честно...средняк, с натяжкой наверное. В общем босса 2-3 только хорошие и приятные. Остальные это какой то в моментах странный мув от разработчиков. К примеру Нюйва и Фуси. Первая фаза максимально скучна, а вторая зависит от рандома как лучезарность из холлоу найта.   Но в целом игра достаточно годная, ибо грамотно расставлены события, диалоги и постановка сюжета в целом. Истинная концовка вообще может вывести на эмоции. Так что - рекомендую!

Writing a review partway through a game isn't usually something I do, but I have some things to get off my mind. I can look back at my critisisms when I finish the game later maybe.  Anyway, spoilers ahead.  Starting with the positives, I can go ON and ON about how good most of this game feels and looks. The artstyle is phenomenal, the concept is brilliant, and the combat is nigh-on perfect in my opinion (aside from the critisisms I have). The story from what I've seen (up to around Jiequan) is pretty cute, I'm fond of some of the characters and their arcs, and I like the whole Yi and Shuanshuan dynamic. It's a very strong metrodvania title all in all.   Anyway, if you're a fan feel free to ridicule me for skill issues, but here are some of my critisisms.  Speaking very generally, the combat feels just slightly not tweaked to perfection. It's really not a major issue for the most part but sometimes it can be a little degrading on my will to finish the game. The one thing I can name off the top of my head is the turnaround time between the charged strike and a parry. My playstyle that I've adapted to uses charged strike a lot, and in tandem with the parry can feel just slightly unfair. I'm trying to deal as much damage as possible, but then the boss hits me with an attack out of the blue while I'm still in a charged strike!! (Skill issue, I know.)  Anyway, some more specific grievances I have come with the bosses themselves. The only one's that have felt fair are Yanlao's claw and the one in the radiant pagoda (can't remember their names.) Goumang sucked in all rights, half the time she's not even attackable, genuinely a miserable time fighting, partly because I'm ass, partly because she simply sucks imo. And (much to the dismay of most players from what I've seen) I dislike Jiequan. That's to say I haven't beaten the fight yet, and this will likely change shortly. I just very much distain the concept of a boss's hp going UP TO MAX in a second phase, rather than just have it happen at half health. I hate this feature, it's ♥♥♥♥ and I hate it.  Also minor gripe, I feel like the dialogue is both inauthentic at times, and can be a little lackluster, which defeats the immersion but is... fine.  Tbh it's still an 8.5/10 game imo, just wanted to vent lmao.

[h2] Неидеальный бред [/h2]  Сегодняшний обзор немного особенный лично для меня, поэтому я бы хотел приступить к основной его части быстрее, чем обычно. Так, смотрим на нормативы, что у нас там: шутка про никому не нужные обзоры, шутка про нонэймовость обозреваемых продуктов, шутка про 1 обзор раз в полтора года, вроде ничего не забыл.  Итак, Nine Sols — замечательная игра, отличный платформер и приключение, съевшее 50 часов моей жизни, стоило ли оно того? [spoiler] Да [/spoiler] Но как описать любовь к этой игре? Возможно объять те чувства и эмоции, что она у меня вызвала? Способен ли мой скромный ум передать через поток несвязного бреда, в чём игра хороша? [spoiler] Нет [/spoiler]  В любом случае я постараюсь.  С игрой я познакомился достаточно давно, помню, что видел её на одной из презентаций, но, как это обычно и бывает, подробности были безвозвратно стёрты потоком времени. Уж не уверен, что конкретно тогда привлекло моё внимание к ней, то ли визуал, то ли сеттинг, или то, что там можно было парировать в воздухе!? Но суть от этого не меняется, я смерть как захотел в это сыграть. У меня горели глаза, а как только в мои руки попала сама игра, то загорелись ещё и мои руки, ибо тот опыт, с которым я прошёл демо, не описать ни с чем. Помню, это был не то вторник, не то понедельник, надо было идти в техан, но меня сразила болезнь, температура под 39, АД как будто только с космоса прилетело, сознание? Какое сознание!? Я там умирал внутри во всех смыслах этого слова! Как бы то ни было, так уж перепало, что в день моей болячки выходила демка 9 Сололололололололо…, и я, не растерявшись, решил воспользоваться этой возможностью, чтобы чекнуть, [i] а стоят ли мои ожидания того? [/i] Да, да, я, великий интеллектуал, вместо того, чтобы нормально отоспаться и попить там чяёчка с лимончиком, витаминов бахнуть, решил, что офигенно будет поместить моё умирающее сознание в обстановку, в которой неплохо было бы сконцентрироваться и быстро реагировать на любые вражеские выпады в игре. Так мало того, я ещё и босса в таком состоянии умудрился убить! [strike] Какой я молодец!:) [/strike] И что по итогу-то? Да ничего особенного, влюбился я с тех пор в игру, увидел, да и отойти от этой любви не могу.  Хорошая история, красивый стиль, запоминающиеся персонажи, классные боссы, [strike] что там ещё принято хвалить, когда нечего сказать? [/strike] Хорошие игры описывать тяжело, любимые ещё сложнее, я буквально не знаю, с чего мне начать? В общем, кажись, я не могу собраться с мыслями, поэтому перейду от предисловия к итогу. Иногда важно ценить хорошо сделанные игры, не гениальные, не идеальные, не прорывные и даже не какие-нибудь ПОМПЕЗНЫЕ игры. Просто хорошие игры, которые не пытаются тебя перегрузить кучей ненужных активностей, тоннами бессмысленного текста, [spoiler] это сейчас был выстрел себе в ногу? 0_о [/spoiler] невообразимыми челенжами, гриндом ради гринда и другими активностями, созданными не столько для того, чтобы доставить мне как игроку удовольствие и приятное время провождение, сколько это самое время украсть.  Nine Sols не идеальная игра, из-за своей линейности это достаточно посредственная метроидвания, с трижды пережёванным арсеналом способностей, который кочует из игры в игру, она имеет бессмысленные механики по типу потери душ из ДС’ов, [strike] нашли что пиздить [/strike], выборов диалогов без собственно выборов, ну и, откровенно говоря, спорную последнюю треть игры. Эта неидеальная игра, которую я люблю, а это неидеальный отзыв, который я написал для этой игры. Обе эти вещи далеки от идеала, но обе из них были сделаны с душой.

I heard Moist call this "game of the year", so I had to try it out, and while I personally wouldn't go that far, it's still very very good.  Having finished Ori and Hollow Knight series, this game felt surprisingly different due to its combat and larger focus on precise parry mechanics. Some of the later bosses are quite challenging and really test your quick reaction skills.  The story, visuals, audio, skill tree, collectibles, puzzles are all very good parts of the experience. [h1]アクション性だけでなくストーリーも傑作[/h1] キーボード操作でストーリーモード200%1/2でトゥルーエンドでクリアした後のレビューです。  [h2]◆総評◆[/h2] 2Dサイドビューメトロイドヴァニア屈指の難易度と知りつつも 評判良いアクションを体験するためにプレイ。操作感も良く グラフィック・タオパンク世界観・壮大なストーリーどれもよくボリュームもかなり多いので大満足です。 開発スタジオはこれが初のメトロイドヴァニア作品とのことですが信じられないくらい素晴らしい出来です。  [h2]◆良かった点◆[/h2] 【ストーリー】 オープニングから復讐を匂わせておりますが、タオパンクの世界観の下 もっと大きな信条・使命感がストーリー進行とともに明らかになってきて引きつけられます。  【キャラクター】 多くを語らない兄貴分な主人公羿と軒軒や思想信条の背景がある九王との掛け合いが良いです。 壮大な物語の中でキャラクター性も立っています。 要所要所のところどころのアメコミ風演出も味があって良いです。  【システム】 パリィで気力を溜めてお札起爆はダメージだけでなく宝石効果で経験値や金のリターンも大きくできるので良いです。 パリィの成功が音で明確に分かるようになっているのでゴチャゴチャするボス戦などで分かりやすいです。 単純に全ての攻撃を防ぐパリィではなく溜や空中、回避などがあり多彩です。 ストーリー進めていくと空中ダッシュや2段ジャンプなど解禁されていきますが 序盤から壁掴みからの挙動とかとにかく多彩で感心します。  【戦闘・難易度】 道中雑魚敵からかなりスリリングですが無視もできるし、溜め攻撃や弓を獲得してからは雑魚戦は楽になります。 予備動作がしっかりあるのでパリィや回避はできるのですが速いのと失敗した時に一気に致命になるのがキツイ。 経験値や金稼ぎしての強化して臨むこともできます。  【グラフィック】 スピーディな戦闘と軽快で滑らかな戦闘アニメーション 細かいしぐさのアニメーション 背景・アイテムなどの作り込みを感じさせる高品質です。  【GBM】 シーンに応じたBGMの変化などで盛り上げてくれます。  [hr][/hr] [h2]◆いまいちな点◆[/h2] [u]次回作が出るなら改善して欲しい[/u]と思うことを記述してます。  【システム】 宝箱を開けるときのカーソル操作でのロック解除 敵の攻撃などがある中ですぐに解除させないとかなら意味ありますが 何もないところで要求してくるのはただの遅延に見えた。  【戦闘・難易度】 高難易度を謳っているタイトルなので覚悟して購入しましたが個人的には難し過ぎました。 ストーリーモードで与ダメ200%、被ダメ1/2くらいでやっと一般メトロイドヴァニアのノーマル～ハードくらいかも？ キャラ強化要素があるにしてもダメージ量に加え敵の攻撃スピード、弾スピードと攻撃の伸びがキツい。 ストーリーモードが無かったらおそらく4～5倍の時間がかるか詰んでしまってオススメしない評価していたと思います。  【UI】 [list] [*]キャラの移動だけでなくメニュー画面もESDFで上下左右操作できるようになってくれれば快適にキーボードのみでも遊べるようになるので少し残念。 (小指を使うWASD操作が苦手なのと左手を1キー分ホームポジションからずらすと感覚が狂うのでどうせならやりたくはない) [*]マップの現時点で通れない場所にどういった要素で通れないのかマークできたら良かった。 [*]神遊でノード間移動ができるようになった時、一度四季閣に戻らないといけないのが手間だった。 [*]ノード移動の時にマップの詳細も見たかった。 [/list]

戦闘システムが良かった。メトロイドヴァニアの2Dアクションとパリィ必須のバランスで何度も死んで覚える歯ごたえのある難易度になっている。探索もアイテムの取得状況などを確認できるようになっているので行き詰まることもなかった。ストーリーも最初は専門用語や謎が多くて全容がつかめないが最後にはすべてが明かされ、理解できるようになるため、2周して改めて会話の内容を確認する楽しさもあった。 ただ残念なのが、メトロイドヴァニアとして探索の楽しさにかけることだ。基本的に一度ボスを攻略したエリアはその時点ですべての探索が可能になってしまうため、新しいアクションを習得した後に再び訪れる必要などがなく、また攻略の順番がほとんど決まっているため、どこを探索しようかと考える必要がない。このあたりが改善できればもっといいゲームになったと思う。

purtroppo mi trovo a doverlo sconsigliare per varie ragioni non banali come sembrano.  1- dialoghi e cut scenes estremamente lunghi.   metà del gameplay sarà ridicolmente interrotto da dialoghi eterni e cutscenes che per quanto siano ben disegnate e animate, impediscono di giocare.  2- poor level design. Praticamente dalla prigione in poi il level design è abbastanza ripetitivo, anche i nemici diventano noiosi.  3- boss design.  dopo goumang, il generale, il robot, il furry con la lancia e la tipa ascetica, gli altri boss sono di un design senza senso che non mi va di stare qui a spiegare e ad analizzare, mi soffermerò solo su quello che per me è stato il boss più NOIOSO di tutti. Einong. Mai visto un boss design più di merda di quello di einong. I movesets sono spesso identici, una quantità di vita infinita per 3 fasi dove devi obbligatoriamente imparare a memoria gli attacchi per counterarli, una boss fight estremamente pilotata e una terza fase che sembra essere studiata per trollare il giocatore con attacchi oggettivamente irreagibili, da parryare a cazzo spammando lb, solo perché sai che arriveranno, ma non perché li vedi. Già le prime due fasi davano segni chiari di quanto sarebbe stato un boss da studiare, con la prima fase di un noioso senza senso, facilissima dopo i primi tentativi, ma impunibile, lenta, una fase praticamente INUTILE, una seconda fase con attacchi impossibili da reagire senza conoscere i move sets a memoria, che tra l'altro hanno sprite oscenamente identici tra attacchi che puniscono se provi a schivare e altri che puniscono se provi a parriare, rendendo l'esperienza di apprendimento di una ritondanza senza senso. La terza fase è quella più imbarazzante, con attacchi umanamente impossibili né da individuare né da reagire, se non conoscendoli a memoria. In tutta la mia vita, non ho mai visto un boss design così di merda, penso il peggiore mai creato in termini di godibilità. Prima di dire che è una skill issue, ricordatevi che la differenza tra un gioco difficile e un gioco di merda è sottile, la bassezza raggiunta con einong è paragonabile ai livelli più "difficili" di geometry dash o di cat mario, non c'è difficoltà, c'è letteralmente da essere trollati. È un peccato che io sia stato costretto dal poor game designing dell'endgame di quest gioco, a dover lasciare una recensione negativa, perché la prima metà del gioco è davvero divertente, i parry sono soddisfacenti, il problema appunto è che dalla prigione in poi inizia il marcio. Il boss meglio riuscito è stato il furry con la lancia. Un altro boss design trash è quello di madame eterea che mi ha fatto ridere per quanto fosse fatto male, infatti ha un paio di cose in comune con einong. Detto questo, il gioco è valido, ma sappiate che verso la fine gli sviluppatori si sono dimostrati incompetenti sia nel game designing sia soprattutto nell'aumentare la difficoltà, perché la difficoltà è sempre la stessa , l'unica cosa ad aumentare sarà la noia assoluta per via dj boss design e level design lenti, ripetitivi e frustranti, che verranno tra l'altro interrotti da dialoghi eterni.   PICCOLA NOTA GRANDISSIMA: So che il gioco tra i suoi difetti soffre dell'assenza di una new game plus, c'è una modalità per rifare i boss... inutile dire che crea dipendenza perchè una volta imparati a memoria, i boss sono veramente divertenti da riaffrontare, tuttavia sarebbero da aggiungere anche i boss secondari e non solo i sols, devo dire che è stata aggiunta frettolosamente questa modalità, come ho trovato frettolose le ultime fasi del gioco. A causa della sua scarsa rigiocabilità, consiglio l'acquisto di questo gioco, che ha comunque più aspetti positivi che negativi, ma a prezzo assolutamente ridotto.

Muy recomendado Lo bueno: Ritmo y fluidez: Es un juego sumamente dinámico que te mantiene enganchado en todo momento. Apartado visual: Cuenta con un arte espectacular y resulta muy atractivo a la vista desde el primer minuto. Narrativa sólida y clara: A diferencia de otros títulos del género, la historia está muy bien llevada. En ningún momento me sentí perdido y siempre entendí perfectamente qué estaba ocurriendo en la trama. Combate excepcional: Las mecánicas son excelentes. Tiene un estilo de combate ágil que te exige reflejos y fomenta la mejora constante como jugador.  Lo menos bueno: Banda sonora: La música acompaña la acción, pero en mi experiencia no logró destacar ni quedarse en mi cabeza. Diseño de entornos: Las zonas del juego cumplen su función, pero no me resultaron súper icónicas ni visualmente memorables.  Conclusión y a quién va dirigido: El juego tiene una progresión bastante lineal y estructurada que recuerda en cierta medida a Hollow Knight, pero donde realmente brilla es en sus peleas, con un sistema de desvíos y choques de espadas fuertemente inspirado en Sekiro. Como punto a tener en cuenta (ni bueno ni malo): no es un juego que permita un desarrollo profundo de builds o personalización de estadísticas. Es una experiencia enfocada en la habilidad pura. Si buscas un combate exigente, basado en reflejos y maestría con la espada, Nine Sols es, sin lugar a dudas, uno de los mejores de su estilo.

[h1]刻意的高难反而磨灭了类银游戏的探索乐趣[/h1]  晚饭的时候刷到了这个游戏的宣传片，看着画风和玩法感觉还不错。发现库里已经有了这个游戏。尝试了一个晚上，给我的感觉实在是一言难尽。  先叠个甲，我不是第一次接触类银的2D闯关类：空洞骑士、神之天平；含rouge元素的小骨、死亡细胞、再早些的Dungreed；国产的也有早期的ICEY与前几年的暗影火炬城都玩过。关于高难动作：法环全成就，只狼仁王和魂123都通关。但综合比下来，就前期这几个小时的体验下来，[b]我很难给这个游戏一个好评。[/b]  抛开美术与世界背景设定不谈，以下只讨论这四个小时里，这个所谓“高难”的类银游戏的游戏性、关卡设计等让我非常不舒服的地方：  [h2]1、跑图成本太高。[/h2] 我不清楚后期的游戏流程是否解锁了各个树点之间的传送，但就前期的游戏体验来说，[b]地图折返的成本实在太高。[/b] 一旦我前期跑图漏了一个地方或者后期解锁了新的机制要回去，只能坐中间的大电梯，再往左右跑很长一段时间的路才能到。 加上每次复活后，路上的怪都会刷新，所以这段回头路并不只是单纯赶路，实际等于要重新清一遍。想直接跑过去也不简单，受击硬直和极短的无敌帧会让整个过程非常折磨。路上还得记得把魂捡了。。  [i]个人认为：发现岔路、隐藏区域，以及通过后期解锁的机制补充前期区域探索，这些原本是类银游戏里非常有吸引力的部分。但在这里，回头探索更像是在重复消耗时间和耐心，总体说极大磨灭了我对地图的探索欲。[/i]  [h2]2、战斗设计死板。[/h2] 我能明显感觉到制作组希望把精防格挡作为主要战斗核心，但实际战斗下来，[b]几乎所有的战斗都在强推甚至逼迫你用精防，不用过不去。[/b]几乎没有的无敌帧和boss的多段攻击让短小的冲刺显的毫无作用。我实际可选的应对方式太少，这导致战斗很容易变得单调。 尤其是打boss英招的时候，可以说我没按过一下普攻或者冲刺，全程LB、RB：精防、起爆来回按，打过的时候没有什么成就感，只感觉按手都酸了——[b]过于单调了。[/b]  [i]个人认为：观察敌人的招式起手、判断攻击节奏，再去选择格挡、闪避、拉开距离或抓后摇反击，这些临场博弈原本是高难类魂游戏里非常吸引人的部分。但在这里，战斗被过度压缩到精防这一种核心解法上，我并没有感觉自己在做什么博弈，只是在按照固定节奏完成输入。[/i]（玩成音游了说是）  [h2]3、关卡设计的细节。[/h2] 打小 Boss 天纲步卫的时候，我真的不理解为什么右边那个会上下移动的箱子不能直接设计成电梯或者绳索。每次从复活点跑过去，箱子往往刚好在往上升，我只能站在原地干等几秒。 更难受的是，天纲步卫的冲刺距离有时候长得离谱，有时候会把我一路顶到右侧。这个时候如果箱子刚好升到上面，我就会因为受击硬直直接掉到下面的通道里。恭喜，这个时候系统判定为脱战boss开始回血，前期全部白打。然后！多半这个时候箱子降下来了，我又得继续等这个箱子升上去，才能重新爬回去。[b]等这个箱子上下乱窜的时间，比我打天纲步卫的时间都长。[/b]  [hr][/hr]  最后，翻看了下这个游戏的成就，这个游戏的前期玩家留存率就更不言而喻了：打败第一个小boss“赤虎刀校-百长”还有83.4%的玩家，到了打败英招就只剩50%左右了。  最后的最后，我其实不太理解为什么要把高难的属性加在一个2D类银游戏里呢？个人认为类银游戏更多的是探图与成长。[b]如果是为了当m，我更愿意直接拿着下位太刀去打激昂金狮子。[/b]

還記得剛出沒多久就買了這款，玩了一個小時多發現不擅長格檔的話會死得很慘， 連小兵群毆都過不了，因此當下果斷退款，直接看實況補劇情了。  過了快兩年，看到特價又手癢，上網看了幾篇心得，找尋如何克服格檔的心理障礙。 結果看到一篇說，玩這款需要放棄洛克人或空洞騎士的習慣，因此自己決定從零開始，好好練習格檔。 沒想到一將原本仙步閃避的習慣都改為格檔，才發現格檔幾乎是無敵的， 就算按的節奏不對，只要有按到基本上不會死。  就這樣，趁著這個勢頭一路破到了易公，才又發現易公明顯強太多，三階要過似乎不太可能。 信心受挫的我，只好忍受屈辱開了劇情模式過真結局。 本來想說就這樣結束了，但心裡卻一直卡著一個疙瘩。 睡了一個晚上，決定再次把遊戲載回來，以交戰回憶模式繼續挑戰易公三階，最後也成功打贏易公一次了。 ------------------------------------ 這款最棒的地方就是見招拆招，最棒的部分就是靠自己掌握到BOSS的攻擊模式，非常有成就感。 從一開始放棄，一直到打贏易公，內心似乎也成長了一些。 感謝赤燭!

Uma história sobre rever os seus próprios princípios e sempre buscar melhorar como pessoa, uma gameplay engajante e que sempre vai se renovando, personagens memoráveis e acima de tudo, um jogo feito com MUITO carinho. Nem lembro quando foi a última vez que fiquei tão viciado em um jogo quanto fiquei com Nine Sols, o início do jogo pode ser meio frustrante até pegar o jeito dos comandos, mas quando você pega não há NADA que te pare ainda mais por ser um jogo que recompensa o jogador mais habilidoso com um dos melhores sound effects de parry que eu já ouvi na minha vida mas que também recompensa o jogador mais casual(eu aí)e que curte ficar explorando o mapa para ver as MUITAS coisas que esse jogo tem a te proporcionar.  A única parte "uim" que eu tenho a comentar do jogo seria sobre a dificuldade não linear dos bosses, alguns são muito fáceis e outros são bem mais difíceis sem você estar preparado, mas isso é normal de metroidvania e não diria que vai afetar EM NADA a gameplay de ninguém, talvez até melhore em algum sentido.  De todo modo eu amei de verdade a experiência que foi jogar Nine Sols e espero que mais pessoas aproveitem a delícia de game que ele é.

메트로베니아를 좋아하는 사람이라면 무조건 해봐야 하는게임. 실크송, 할로우나이트와 비교해도 밀리지 않는 게임이라고 생각합니다. 전투난이도는 실크송보다 높고 플랫포밍 난이도는 낮은편입니다. 전투난이도가 높은 이유는 패링의 활용이 핵심이기 때문입니다. 그러나 이건 그렇게 큰 압박은 아닙니다. 패링을 해야한다는 압박감때문에 꺼리는 분들이 많을것같은데 정말정말 널럴한 타이밍이고 한시간이면 손에 익어요. 결론적으로 플랫포밍과 같이보면 실크송과 난이도가 비슷합니다. 피로도는 적은 편이에요. 맵이 악의없이 짜여있고 보스들은 빠르지만 정박패턴을 구사합니다. 난이도가 정말 높은 보스도 계속 머리박으면 결국 이길수 있어요.  레벨디자인은 캐릭터 성장보다는 플레이어 성장에 맞춰져 있습니다. 꼭한번해보시길.. 후회없을거라고 생각해요.

difficult but rewarding game with beautiful art and great environmental worldbuilding. i dont think people actually give the story enough credit for having a really solidly written history that connects with the present dilemmas the game presents you with. most of the sols also felt very distinct in their designs and motivations, as well as their boss fights which i appreciated considering theres so many of them. the drawn panels during cutscenes made the story even more immersive and enhanced the impact of emotional moments too. i also liked how you can feel yi's gradual character arc despite there not being very dialogue-heavy scenes since everything is written with purpose. i honestly really enjoyed most of the puzzles involving the mystic nymph or different abilities you had to unlock.  about the parry mechanics: anyone complaining about the parry system is wrong.   youre annoyed you misjudged what type of attack was coming and you died? skill issue. youre frustrated that often the actual attack is delayed after when you think the boss will hit you? skill issue. youre pissed the parry window is too small? skill issue.   people when they buy a hard game and then get mad that its hard at first :/ i think the three different types of parries are pretty well balanced and this is clearly a game that makes you parry because it wants to force the player to improve rather than try to grind for overpowered upgrades as a crutch. in that sense i really enjoyed boss fights even when i was doing terribly because it always came down to skill, which youll naturally improve if you keep at it. its intended to be a hard game, but its not an unforgiving one. losing gold can be frustrating but its very easy to accumulate more and you dont lose items that are worth more anyway, and boss fights are always near a root node. its easy to retry but it only depends on whether the player is willing to, which if you are not you shouldnt have even bought the game and complained because EVERYONE warned you lol.  anyways 10/10 game would recommend to everyone who likes a challenge or hates themselves a little.

As a fan of metroidvanias and difficult but fair games, I have to say Nine Sols was a masterpiece.   Positives: The story is beautiful and is discovered naturally by returning to the base. I didn't have to go out of my way to discover most of the interactions. And many kept me engaged in the exploration sections. Bosses are well choreographed. They tell you what they'll do and you always have time to react, however, you do have to look for the tells or you'll get destoryed.  Negatives: Almost nothing, if you like challenges. My own view probebly isn't that common but I found some parries too simple. There were two bosses that took me about two hours, even on the true ending, but once you've learnt the timing anything can be parried. Even the red attacks, which spooked me at first since I also played Sekiro.

Nine Sols é o melhor metroidvania que já joguei — na minha opinião, até melhor que Hollow Knight. O combate é excelente, a história é muito boa e ainda conta com alguns pequenos plot twists interessantes.  No quesito exploração, acredito que ele fique um pouco atrás de Hollow Knight, mas não me entendam mal: a exploração continua sendo muito boa. Os mapas são bem interconectados, e as lutas contra os chefes estão entre as melhores que já vi no gênero.  De forma geral, os bosses “ensinam” o jogador ao longo das lutas, mostrando gradualmente como enfrentá-los. A dificuldade escala de maneira natural, fazendo com que cada vitória seja extremamente satisfatória.  O boss final é um verdadeiro desafio, exigindo frieza, paciência e prática. Não é impossível, porém: é preciso entender quais são as poucas aberturas do chefe e puni-las rapidamente.  Excelente jogo! Valeu muito a pena a compra.

This game isnt completely perfect, it has some flaws, but regardless this was a 10/10 for me.     I will start with some of its flaws first.   First, I enjoyed the map, I liked exploring around New Kunlun and learning how it came to be, the only flaw is that early on its not clear where you should be going, you may find yourself lost at some point unless unless you can remember where you can go now that you unlocked a new ability. I generally liked exploring the map so I didnt mind as much but I did have some moments where I was just walking around. It doesnt help you dont get fast travel until around half way through the game so you'll be walking around a fair bit. Whats also weird was the boss fights, some boss fights was easy, but some are crazy hard. I did the 3rd boss which was relatively easy but when i got to the 4th it felt like i jumped difficulties by 2. I really struggling with that boss and when i finally completed it, the bosses that came after felt loads easier (besides final)  The next is the parry system, no matter how you go about the game you need to be able to parry and im a bit mixed on it with some issues I have.   It can be really difficult to tell when the parry timing is right depending on the monsters or bosses you face, each one has its own distinct timing but its not consistent with how you know its coming. for example, monster A might want you to parry as its arm is swinged down whilst the other is half way because he's faster. This is fine to an extent but theres no real indicator for you, you just have to go by feel which can be brutal because you will essentially need to die for you to get use to it. This is fairly normal in a souls like game though so I wasnt too fussed about this         My second issue is the varying parry mechanics, there are 3 to keep in mind normal attack - parry normally red attack - jump and parry projectile red attack - hold down parry button to deflect  problem 1 is that both red attack and project attack are red when the bosses, monsters start there animation which becomes a problem with guessing which parry to use. It felt less like skill and more "learn there attack pattern" so you learn from that compared to having indicators. There are very small things you can notice to help you predict whats coming, but it felt annoying at times when what i thought was a projectile was actually a normal slash and i die.     Second problem is the projectile red attack. to parry them you have to hold down the parry button which is annoying if you realise its not actually a projectile by the end. It leaves you little space to correct yourself so you really need to make sure its actually a projectile coming or you will get slashed.  Now onto the pro's as I have stated that there was problem with the combat, it was pretty consistent so once you get use to it you will end up enjoying the combat a lot. Considering I played Sekiro and found the combat boring I was surprised to enjoy it loads more in Nine Sols. Despite its flaws it never felt clunky or unreliable, just frustrating in tough fights but there was usually an easier or efficient way once you figured it out.      As said earlier, i thoroughly enjoy the map mainly because of the world building. I think they did a really good job making an interesting world to learn about and keeps you invested into wanting to learn more. Its not for everyone ofc, but if you enjoy sci/fi - cyberpunk esque world with taosim being influenced. You will enjoy it  The Sound track wins bonus points as being really well made. really fit the game perfectly  Lastly and most importantly why this game was a 10/10 is the story. I really enjoyed to say the least, it fits with the world perfectly and what Red Candle Games were aiming for. Its not just another fantasy world to explore, but on a ship with unique ideas put together held by a great story which all links together flawlessly. This is by far one of the better world building games I've played and why its a 10/10. Before i got the the final boss I was contemplating to place it as a 9 for its flaws I highlighted. But by the end I realised im going to miss playing this game and clearly the flaws didnt hinder how much I enjoyed this game

Un buen juego, hace muchas cosas excelentes y es divertido en muchas ocasiones. Pero las fallas son tan frustrantes que le tengo que dar el voto negativo. De verdad quería que este fuera "EL METROIDVANIA", pero no. El Lore es increíble pero la jugabilidad no le hace justicia.  - Logros y contenido perdible sin que se te avise, (Ej: Logro: ¿Qué has hecho?) - Hay un pickup escondido que el juego no te muestra de ninguna manera, solo puedes saber que esta ahi mediante una guia (Periodico academico de Tiandao) - Buena parte de los bosses tiene una fase final tan dificil que terminas haciendo la fase inicial no hit de tanto repetir, es una perdida de tiempo. - El boss Ji se buggea, se queda quieto si le das con el arco antes de que comience a rerrollear su proximo ataque y no hace nada hasta que le matas. Se queda en el aire con un sprite de noqueo hasta morir. - Lady Eterea es un jefe que depende de la suerte en su fase 2, cualquier clon puede ser ella, seria genial poder contar cuantos clones falsos pasan para deducir cual es la real. Por desgracia, no es asi y puedes darle hasta a 6 sombras seguidas sin infligirle daño al jefe. - Los jades apenas marcan una diferencia en el gameplay, pasarse el juego con o sin ellos es una experiencia similar. - El juego se olvida muy rapido de la mecanica de la "Ninfa Mistica", en las ultimas zonas del juego no sirve para nada, parece que se olvidaron de implementarla. - Lore: Compañeros de Lear, apenas recuerdo algo de los compañeros/seguidores de Lear, y se supone que son importantes para la historia. Hasta tienen puzzles dedicados de forma individual. - Al final del juego, tener 1 de vida como tener 30000 es lo mismo. Todo jefe te mata exactamente de 2 golpes, la cosa es rezar para esquivar el segundo una vez te de el primero porque:   - NO HAY TIEMPO DE INVULNERABILIDAD ENTRE HIT Y HIT, lo que hace que varios enemigos o varios ataques consecutivos te hagan INSTAKILL porque TE NOQUEAN ENTRE HIT Y HIT, MORIRAS DE FORMA INSTANTANEA INCLUSO SI LLEVAS EL JADE DE RESURRECCION :] - El mapa: Es basura. Busca una guia o instala un mod que te de la ubicacion de lo que te resta por recolectar porque este te confudira, hay AREAS BLOQUEADAS QUE SE MUESTRAN ACCESIBLES, eso te hara dar un buen par de vueltas, ¿Te imaginas un mapa que marque las puertas cerradas? - ¿Recuerdas que en la primera cinemática del juego te muestran a Yi moribundo y tu no sabes el contexto de esa situación? Bueno, el juego te lo dirá en la ultima media hora de las 40 que suele durar la partida, espero que recuerdes la escena para entonces :] - Los sprites de ataque de la jefa final están bug, hay golpes que pega con Bluetooth.   No lo recomiendo simplemente por el amargo gusto que me deja la jugabilidad en la boca, y la pelea final, de lo peor, una cinemática de Yi destrozando a su mentora era mejor elección.

8/10  [hr][/hr]  优秀的类银只狼。  [hr][/hr]  弹反反馈感非常强，与之相对的是被打乒乓球时的挫败与无力，但瑕不掩瑜。  地图过于扁平化，以古树为中心一路平铺，多周目探索会非常乏味几乎只是为了Boss Rush或是补成就。  美术以及音乐非常让人惊艳，蚨蝶跳跳乐以及之后的Boss战都是顶中顶，虽然听不懂太阳语但ED曲就是不知道在好听几点。  单把剧情拎出来看倒是中规中矩，但人物塑造和演出又弥补了这一点，羿恒兄妹两人真的太可爱了，每每收到恒的讯息，进入与恒的回忆时，嘴角就不自觉的上扬，有这么一位善解人意的妹妹试问谁不心动？抛开个人感情不谈，两人的描写其实为后续的关键抉择埋下了伏笔，在不断“射日”的途中到了解到“太阳”们不为人知的过往，与轩轩这位曾被自己视为耗材的“猿人”培养出的羁绊，以上这些都在真结局演出时，酝酿出一颗无与伦比的情感炸弹。毫无疑问，九日的剧情设计是成功的。  总结一下，类只狼银河城可以说是非常新颖，独一档的体验。非常推荐适应只狼打铁且有类银经验的玩家游玩，即使类银苦手也可以剧情模式体验九日的优秀剧情。

I've seen a lot of people recommend this game for HK and SS players and for that I say: It's not the same. I reckon the only similarity is its metroidvania genre and boss battles but, in terms of story, lore, fight mechanics and gameplay, Nine Sols is its own beast that are more akin to games that focus more on combat than exploration.  It's definitely Sekiro themed and inspired heavy because its entire gameplay focuses on parry, giving it a harder difficulty curve for people who are more used to dodging and dashing through attacks as you have to face the attack directly to be able to parry. Though, it's parry window is way more generous than most games.  The way a friend of mine put it is that while HK focuses attack and exploration, Nine Sols focuses on defense and combat.  Overall, my personal thoughts is that this game is a must-have because it's story is SO good and its gameplay is pretty fun once you get into the groove of parrying. The bosses are definitely a challenge at first but once you notice their patterns and tells, they're a fun fight. 10/10.

JUEGARDO, JUEGARDO, JUEGARDOOOOOOOO.  Entré esperando un metroidvania “normalito” para relajarme un rato y terminé completamente enganchado. La mecánica de parry es, sin duda, lo mejor del juego: se siente precisa, satisfactoria y hace que cada combate tenga muchísima intensidad. Cuando le agarras el ritmo, parece que estuvieras jugando una coreografía perfecta de golpes y reflejos.  Todo lo demás también está a un nivel altísimo. El mundo, los escenarios y los personajes transmiten muchísimo cariño y personalidad, tomando inspiración clara de Hollow Knight, pero logrando identidad propia. Artísticamente es un 10/10: la mezcla de estética tao-punk con la ambientación y la música crean una atmósfera increíble.  En gameplay también sobresale muchísimo. No llega al nivel de dificultad de Hollow Knight 1 siquiera, pero sinceramente no le hace falta. El sistema de combate está tan bien diseñado que cada pelea se siente emocionante y súper satisfactoria.  Excelente juego. Totalmente vale la pena. Digno de estar con los grandes.

Nine Sols is a truly special game; it does not hold back with the story it wishes to tell, and pulls no punches with its gameplay. When I was a young child, I was given the Tao Te Ching to read and told to absorb its message every day. Through a process of something called life, I took many years to consume the wise words that were written in that book. But having learned of the Tao and its ways, I was truly able to see all the brilliance and influence it had over this game. The philosophy behind certain characters and their motivations, Lear feeling like he was inspired by Laozi himself, the very clear tai chi moves Yi knows and learns more of. Even the root being somehow inspired by or is a representation of the yin and yang itself was all so wonderful and a truly magical experience that I was not ready for.     To add further, even the fighting within the gameplay is steeped in the ways of the Tao. If you fight against it, and try just dodging or doing something other than what it wants you to do, you will fail. But- if you learn to parry, learn the timing, and breathe and flow with the game, you will accomplish all that Yi sets out to do, and become one with each other. After all, "Knowing others is intelligence; knowing yourself is true wisdom. Mastering others is strength; mastering yourself is true power."   I have to commend everyone that helped make this game a reality. Because there's only so many truly moving games that come through life and will leave an impression on you. I cannot fully imply or explain what this game did or helped me through, but as stated before, certain things in life like movies or games or stories with ideas and philosophy will affect you. This is what art is, and what it should continue to be.  After all, "A good traveler has no fixed plans and is not intent upon arriving. A good artist lets their intuition lead them wherever it wants. A good scientist has freed themselves of concepts and keeps their mind open to what is. Thus the master is available to all people and doesn't reject anyone. They are ready to use all situations and doesn't waste anything. This is called embodying the light."  So if there's any of the developers, or people who helped make this game reading this, I want to genuinely say, from the bottom of my heart, thank you. Thank you for all your work and time and effort. From at least this one person, thank you <3

I should've written a review for this game long ago, but at least now I don't have recency bias. With that in mind, I can say that Nine Sols is, without a doubt, my favorite game of all time. This game's only major flaw, in my opinion, is the exploration being mostly linear, and even that hardly bothered me at all. Everything else is nearly perfect--the engaging, parry focused combat (this game converted me to parryism), which has the most fun bosses I've ever fought; the beautiful and unique Taopunk asthetic with breathtaking (and frequently very violent) hand-drawn art; the music, providing a fitting and oftentimes very deeply emotional background to every moment (seriously, I cry just listening to the soundtrack sometimes); and most of all, the story. Nine Sols has possibly the most powerful and touching story I've ever had the privilege of playing through. It is existential, thoughtful, heartbreaking, shocking, and beautiful. Every character has depth, and you get to know and understand all of them. Watching Yi grow as a person in real time is such a moving experience, and discovering the tragic tale of New Kunlun through both environmental storytelling and Yi's memories as you slowly begin to understand the history of the land that betrayed you is such a gripping journey. The biggest complaint I see about this game is the difficulty, but they added a story mode to mitigate specifically that. Red Candle Games has crafted a masterpiece, and I am happy to have had the honor of playing it.   *You have no more unread messages from Penglai*

Soulslikes tend to artificially extend gameplay by presenting things you just don't or can't know as "difficulty", and I don't think this game is any different. It's tiresome being punished for not making the correct decision the first time you encounter something.  As an example, in the opening level there's this elevator to hack and then a path into the basement behind it. I had thought the basement was a secret and the elevator was the way forward (there's nothing to indicate which is the way forward), so I went to the basement first, and cos I made that decision I couldn't get back up to the elevator to check the secret when I realised the basement is actually the way forward. Punished for not knowing which way was correct. Can't restart the level. Have to restart the game to check that secret? Nah, don't think so.  Combat looks like it will extend more later in the game, but the basic combat you begin with is just this silly parry system where it's a better idea to put yourself in danger to make the parry and retaliate instead of just dodging. I think this is bad game design. And this is the artificial gameplay extension I'm talking about cos you'll get punished for not trying to parry and you'll be punished for incorrect parry timing, unless it is a specific attack you cannot parry and are supposed to dodge.  The story seems like it might turn into something interesting, but I don't think the gameplay is good enough to warrant getting into the story, and while there is a "story mode" difficult, it would be quite boring to gut this game of it's core mechanics and turn it into a walking simulator. So your choices are a false sense of difficulty or no difficulty.

As a tl;dr, I will say this was a great game and totally worth your time if you love metroidvanias and a good challenge.  Now, for a more in-depth review, feel free to keep on reading (I'll also try to keep it as spoiler free as possible).  To start things off, the gameplay. It took me a good bit to really get a good feel for the proper flow of combat and how to properly deal with most enemies, but it was a grand time when I did. Even getting up to that point and even dealing with some of the frustration of the harder enemies and bosses, for the most part, it was nice feeling myself getting wiser and more adept at parrying and properly countering enemies. Speaking of parrying and countering, I do enjoy the system in place in the game and the couple of different ways of going about it in later sections of the game. One drawback I can offer here, and this is just personal preference: I wish your basic attacks had more bite by end-game. Even after collecting everything and maxing the skill tree, it just felt like stabbing late game bosses with a toothpick. I get the focus is on countering, but having some means of further boosting basic attacks would have been appreciated. As a slight side note, I do greatly appreciated that the game's "easy mode" allows you to set how much damage you do and how the enemies do to you, making it EXTREMELY player accessible and even making extra runs for achievements a breeze.  Another little tidbit involving bosses is the intros. Naturally, upon meeting a boss for the first time, you'll be given a little scene with them, but that's not what I'm talking about. The one I'm talking about is when you die to said boss and go back to retry the fight. This is obviously a personal gripe, so take it with a grain of salt, but dying multiple times to a single boss, only to run back to their room and wait X amount of seconds just to get rolling again really wore on me for the tougher or longer fights. In a related note, it would have been nice if the game had a "skip cutscene" function, not just for these intros, but for repeat playthroughs where you're going speedrunner mode or you're just running through for a few achievements you missed.  Going away from criticism for a minute, I will say the plot, lore and world-building were all superbly done in my book. I genuinely cared for the characters that you come across and interact with in the game and only wanted to see the best for all of the friendly crew (and even a couple of the antagonists). The music, although not standing out for the most part, definitely did the job well of properly matching the vibe each area or scene was going for. Speaking of areas, I feel the map as a whole was quite enjoyable to explore and maneuver through and the amount of collectables hit just the right amount; not too many and not too little. It was also a nice feature to give the player more reason to explore to find a "particular way" to show how much you've found in a given area.  I will say though, just as a personal gripe (once again), later on when you are given the ability to traverse from checkpoint to checkpoint, I find it a little silly you can only do so from the main hub area.   I could go on, but those are just the main points here. Once again, great game, very challenging, really fun and only has a few things I can gripe about. Kudos to Red Candle for making this and I look forward to what you bring to the table next time!
